NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT
CONTRACT AGREEMENT
AGREEMENT NUMBER: NISD-2024-001
DATE OF EXECUTION: 2023-10-12

THIS AGREEMENT is made and entered into this 12th day of October, 2023, by and between NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT, a political subdivision of the State of Texas (hereinafter referred to as the "District"), and the NORTHWOOD EDUCATION ASSOCIATION, a labor organization (hereinafter referred to as the "Association").

WHEREAS, the District desires to employ certified personnel to provide educational services to the students of the District; and

WHEREAS, the Association represents the certified employees of the District; and

WHEREAS, the parties desire to establish the terms and conditions of employment for the certified employees;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

ARTICLE I: DEFINITIONS

1.1 "Certified Employee" shall mean any employee of the District holding a valid teaching certificate issued by the Texas Education Agency.
1.2 "Fiscal Year" shall mean the period from September 1 of any year to August 31 of the following year.
1.3 "Academic Year" shall mean the period from the first day of instruction in September to the last day of instruction in June.
1.4 "Salary Schedule" shall mean the compensation scale attached hereto as Exhibit A.
1.5 "Benefits" shall mean the health, dental, and retirement benefits attached hereto as Exhibit B.

ARTICLE II: TERM

2.1 This Agreement shall become effective on July 1, 2024 (hereinafter referred to as the "Effective Date").
2.2 The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of 3 Years (hereinafter referred to as the "Contract Term").
2.3 The Contract Term shall expire on June 30, 2027.
2.4 Either party may provide written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the Contract Term.
2.5 If no notice of non-renewal is provided, this Agreement shall automatically renew for an additional 1 Year period.

ARTICLE III: COMPENSATION

3.1 Base Salary.
3.1.1 The District shall pay the base salary of each Certified Employee according to the Salary Schedule set forth in Exhibit A.
3.1.2 The Salary Schedule shall reflect a salary increase of 4.5% for all steps on the schedule effective on the Effective Date.
3.1.3 The salary increase shall be prorated for any employee commencing employment after the Effective Date.
3.2 Longevity Pay.
3.2.1 Employees with five (5) or more years of service in the District shall receive a longevity pay increment of $500 per year.
3.2.2 Employees with ten (10) or more years of service in the District shall receive a longevity pay increment of $1,000 per year.
3.3 Stipends.
3.3.1 Stipends for coaching, advising, and department chairperson duties shall be paid in accordance with District Policy 3.10.
3.3.2 Stipends shall be paid on a monthly basis throughout the school year.

ARTICLE IV: WORKING CONDITIONS

4.1 Work Day.
4.1.1 The standard work day for Certified Employees shall be eight (8) hours.
4.1.2 The standard work week shall be five (5) days.
4.2 Class Size.
4.2.1 Class sizes shall not exceed thirty (30) students per classroom for elementary grades.
4.2.2 Class sizes shall not exceed thirty-five (35) students per classroom for secondary grades.
4.3 Planning Time.
4.3.1 Each Certified Employee shall receive at least one (1) planning period per day.
4.3.2 Planning periods shall not be assigned to non-instructional duties.

ARTICLE V: PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT

5.1 District Support.
5.1.1 The District shall allocate funds for professional development activities.
5.1.2 Funds shall be allocated based on the number of Certified Employees employed.
5.2 Leave for Professional Development.
5.2.1 Certified Employees may take up to five (5) days of professional development leave per year.
5.2.2 Professional development leave shall be with full compensation.
5.2.3 Professional development leave must be approved by the Superintendent.

ARTICLE VI: DISPUTE RESOLUTION

6.1 Grievance Procedure.
6.1.1 Grievances shall be processed in accordance with the District Grievance Procedure.
6.1.2 All grievances must be submitted in writing within ten (10) days of the incident.
6.2 Arbitration.
6.2.1 Disputes regarding the interpretation of this Agreement shall be submitted to arbitration.
6.2.2 The arbitrator shall be selected by mutual agreement of the parties.
6.2.3 The decision of the arbitrator shall be final and binding.

ARTICLE VII: TERMINATION

7.1 Termination for Cause.
7.1.1 The District may terminate employment for cause in accordance with state law.
7.1.2 Cause includes insubordination, negligence, or violation of District Policy.
7.2 Termination without Cause.
7.2.1 The District may terminate employment without cause upon payment of severance pay.
7.2.2 Severance pay shall be equal to one (1) month of salary for each year of service.
7.3 Resignation.
7.3.1 Certified Employees shall provide thirty (30) days written notice of resignation.
7.3.2 Failure to provide notice may result in forfeiture of unused sick leave.

ARTICLE VIII: GENERAL PROVISIONS

8.1 Governing Law.
8.1.1 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Texas.
8.2 Severability.
8.2.1 If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid, the remainder shall continue in full force.
8.3 Entire Agreement.
8.3.1 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties.
8.4 Amendments.
8.4.1 Amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties.
8.5 Notices.
8.5.1 All notices shall be sent via certified mail to the addresses listed in this Agreement.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement on the date first written above.

FOR THE DISTRICT:

_____________________________
SUPERINTENDENT R. WILSON
NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT
DATE: 2023-10-12

FOR THE ASSOCIATION:

_____________________________
UNION REP S. MARTINEZ
NORTHWOOD EDUCATION ASSOCIATION

LEGAL OPINION MEMORANDUM
TO: BOARD OF TRUSTEES, NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT
FROM: J. MILLER, LEGAL COUNSEL
DATE: 2023-10-10
SUBJECT: LEGAL REVIEW OF CONTRACT RATIFICATION

This memorandum serves as the legal opinion regarding the proposed Contract Agreement between the District and the Northwood Education Association.

1.0 AUTHORITY TO NEGOTIATE
The District has the authority to negotiate and enter into collective bargaining agreements with its employees pursuant to Texas Education Code Section 21.001 et seq.

2.0 COMPLIANCE WITH STATUTE
The proposed agreement complies with all applicable state statutes. The salary increase of 4.5% is within the budgetary limits approved by the Board. The contract term of 3 Years is consistent with standard practice in the region. The effective date of 2024-07-01 aligns with the fiscal year start.

3.0 FUNDING
The necessary funds for the salary increase and benefits are available in the District budget. The financial impact statement attached hereto confirms the availability of funds.

4.0 PROCEDURAL COMPLIANCE
The negotiation process followed all required public notice procedures. The Board meeting on 2023-10-12 provided the required opportunity for public comment. The vote result of 4-1 meets the threshold for adoption.

5.0 RECOMMENDATION
Based on the review, it is the opinion of Legal Counsel that the Contract Agreement is valid and enforceable. The Board is advised to proceed with ratification.

RESPECTFULLY SUBMITTED,
J. MILLER
LEGAL COUNSEL

Current total salary expense for certified employees is $15,000,000.

Proposed total salary expense for certified employees is $15,675,000.

The total financial impact is $810,000.

The 3 Years contract term results in a total commitment of $2,430,000 over the life of the agreement.
This commitment is within the long-term fiscal plan.

8.0 CONCLUSION
The District has the financial capacity to absorb the proposed increase.

NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT

A RESOLUTION OF THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT AUTHORIZING THE RATIFICATION OF THE COLLECTIVE BARGAINING AGREEMENT WITH THE NORTHWOOD EDUCATION ASSOCIATION.

WHEREAS, the Board of Trustees of the Northwood Independent School District (the "Board") has engaged in negotiations with the Northwood Education Association (the "Association") regarding the terms and conditions of employment for certified employees; and

WHEREAS, the parties have reached a tentative agreement on the terms of a new Collective Bargaining Agreement; and

WHEREAS, the Board desires to ratify the agreement and authorize the Superintendent to execute the same;

NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT AS FOLLOWS:

SECTION 1. RATIFICATION.
The Board hereby ratifies the Collective Bargaining Agreement between the District and the Association, effective July 1, 2024, for a term of 3 Years.

SECTION 2. AUTHORIZATION.
The Board hereby authorizes Superintendent R. Wilson to execute the Agreement on behalf of the District.

SECTION 3. UNIONS REPRESENTATIVE.
The Board acknowledges Union Representative S. Martinez as the signatory for the Association.

SECTION 4. EFFECTIVE DATE.
The Agreement shall become effective on 2024-07-01.

SECTION 5. SALARY INCREASE.
The Agreement includes a salary increase of 4.5%.

SECTION 6. ADOPTION.
This Resolution shall be adopted by a vote of 4-1.

SECTION 7. EFFECTIVE DATE OF RESOLUTION.
This Resolution shall take effect immediately upon adoption.

PASSED AND ADOPTED at a regular meeting of the Board of Trustees of the Northwood Independent School District held on October 12, 2023.

CITY OF MILLBROOK
ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS
REGULAR MEETING MINUTES

MEETING ID: ZBA-2024-06-28
DATE: June 28, 2024
LOCATION: City Hall Room 204
SUBJECT: Regular Meeting Minutes - June 28, 2024
AUTHOR: Zoning Board of Appeals
RECIPIENTS: City Clerk, Public Record

PROCEEDINGS OF THE REGULAR MEETING OF THE ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS OF THE CITY OF MILLBROOK HELD ON THE 28TH DAY OF JUNE, 2024, AT THE CITY HALL ROOM 204, 100 MAIN ST, MILLBROOK.

CALL TO ORDER
The meeting was called to order at 6:00 PM by Chairperson David Rivera. The Chairperson noted that the meeting was convened pursuant to the authority granted under Chapter 14 of the Millbrook Municipal Code. The Chairperson announced the availability of the Webcast Available: Yes for the public viewing via the City of Millbrook official website portal.

ATTENDANCE AND ROLL CALL
The following members of the Zoning Board of Appeals were present:
Member Marcus Patterson
Member Elena Kowalski
Member David Rivera
Member Sarah Chen
Alternate Member: None

City Staff Present:
ZBA Clerk: Present
Administrative Assistant: Recorder
Legal Counsel: Present
Planning Department Representative: Present

The Chairperson declared that a Quorum Established was present.

ROLL CALL OF MEMBERS
The Secretary called the roll.
Member Marcus Patterson: Present
Member Elena Kowalski: Present
Member David Rivera: Present
Member Sarah Chen: Present
Member: Absent
Member: Absent

The Chairperson confirmed the presence of four (4) voting members, constituting a quorum. The Recorder noted the Audio File: Recorded and Video File: Recorded for the proceedings. The Recorder noted that the Transcript: Pending.

CONSENT AGENDA
The Board considered the Consent Agenda. No items were presented for discussion or objection. The motion to approve the Consent Agenda was made and seconded. The vote was unanimous.

REGULAR ORDER OF BUSINESS

ITEM 1: VARIANCE REQUEST 24-011
APPLICANT: Millbrook Community Land Trust
PROPERTY ADDRESS: 1600 Community Drive
REQUEST TYPE: Setback Variance
ZONING DISTRICT: R-1

The Board considered the Land Trust application. The Staff Report ID: PD-2024-06-20 was reviewed by the Board. The Staff Recommendation: APPROVE was read into the record by the Planning Department Representative. The representative outlined the technical specifications of the proposed project.

PROJECT DETAILS
The project is classified as Project Type: Affordable Housing. The proposal includes a Unit Count: 40 residential units. The property is located within the R-1 Zoning District. The Federal Grant ID: FHA-2024-99 was cited in the application documentation as the funding source for the development.

PUBLIC COMMENT
The Chairperson opened the Public Comment section. The Public Comment Log: Attached was referenced. A total of Public Speakers: 15 individuals registered to speak. The following list represents the speakers who addressed the Board during this session:

1.  James Henderson, 1605 Community Drive, Support
2.  Linda Martinez, 1599 Community Drive, Opposition
3.  Robert Smith, 1610 Community Drive, Neutral
4.  Susan Williams, 1602 Community Drive, Support
5.  Michael Brown, 1595 Community Drive, Opposition
6.  Jennifer Davis, 1608 Community Drive, Support
7.  Thomas Wilson, 1601 Community Drive, Opposition
8.  Patricia Moore, 1604 Community Drive, Support
9.  Charles Taylor, 1606 Community Drive, Opposition
10. Nancy Anderson, 1603 Community Drive, Support
11. Daniel Jackson, 1607 Community Drive, Opposition
12. Karen White, 1609 Community Drive, Support
13. Mark Harris, 1611 Community Drive, Opposition
14. Elizabeth Martin, 1612 Community Drive, Support
15. Richard Thompson, 1613 Community Drive, Opposition

The comments submitted by the public were noted in the official log. The Community Reaction: Strong Opposition was recorded in the staff summary based on the volume and nature of the comments received. The Media Coverage: Local News was noted as a factor in the public awareness of this hearing.

STAFF PRESENTATION AND TECHNICAL REVIEW
The Planning Department Representative presented the Staff Report ID: PD-2024-06-20. The report detailed the zoning compliance issues associated with the proposed construction. The Board considered the Land Trust application regarding the variance request.

During the technical review, the Board reviewed the setback calculations submitted with the application. It was noted that the Setback calculation was found to be non-compliant. The technicality cited was specifically regarding the Setback Calculation required by Section 5-102 of the Municipal Code. The Applicant Response: Pending was recorded regarding the request for clarification on the specific measurements.

MEMBER DISCUSSION

1.0 ADMINISTRATIVE OVERVIEW

This memorandum serves as the official staff report for the Zoning Board of Appeals regarding the application submitted by Crestview Holdings LLC. The Planning Department has completed the staff review process. The review date for the initial assessment was July 9, 2024. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete. The application details involve a request for Variance for Density and Variance for Parking. The project name associated with this request is Riverfront Luxury Apartments. The property address is 1500 River Road. The parcel identification number is 14-05-22-200. The zoning district is designated as R-1. The proposed unit count is 200. The principal applicant is Marcus Webb. The staff reviewer assigned to this matter is James Wilson.

2.0 APPLICATION SUMMARY

Crestview Holdings LLC submitted a request for variance. The application was received by the Planning Department. Staff review completed the necessary analysis of the submitted plans. The recommendation drafted by the staff is to DENY the request. The review status is Final. The documentation is Complete. The reasoning is Unchanged. The pattern is Consistent. The third recommendation is DENY. The second recommendation is Yes. The previous recommendation was DENY.

3.0 TECHNICAL ANALYSIS

3.1 Parking Standards
The application requires a variance for Parking. Parking calculations do not meet R-1 standards. The number of proposed units exceeds the capacity of the designated parking structure. The deficiency relates to the Insufficient Parking provision within the R-1 zoning code. This finding is consistent with previous staff observations.

3.2 Density Standards
The application requires a variance for Density. Density is incompatible with surrounding character. The proposed unit count of 200 units significantly alters the residential density of the area. The surrounding character does not support this level of intensity. This finding is consistent with previous staff observations.

3.3 Traffic Impact
The application requires review of Traffic Impact Study Deficiencies. The Traffic Impact Study Deficiencies have been identified as a New Deficiency. The study submitted does not account for peak hour traffic volumes associated with 200 units. The study fails to meet the requirements set forth in the municipal traffic code. This deficiency was identified during the Review Date of July 9, 2024.

4.0 PROCEDURAL STATUS

The Application received was processed through the standard intake protocol. The Staff review completed the verification of all submitted documents. The Recommendation drafted was reviewed by the Legal Department. The Legal Review is Complete. The Report filed is part of the official record. The Meeting scheduled is set for the Public Hearing Date of July 15, 2024. The Meeting Time is 6:00 PM. The Room is City Hall 204. The Agenda Item is 4. The Attachments total 15 Documents. The Distribution list includes Board Members. Copies were sent to the City Clerk. Copies were sent to the Applicant.

5.0 PUBLIC NOTICE AND NOTIFICATION

The Public Notice was Posted in accordance with municipal code. The Board Notification was Sent to all members. The Public Interest is High. The Media Attention is Local. The Applicant Response is None. The Review Status is Final. The Priority is High. The Tracking ID is TRK-2024-710. The Reference Number is VAR-24-010. The Page Count is 8 pages. The Filing Location is Planning Files. The Record Type is Official Memo. The Confidentiality is None.

6.0 CONCLUSION

The recommendation is to DENY the request. The reasons for this recommendation are Insufficient Parking, Density Incompatible, and Traffic Impact Study Deficiencies. The Staff Reviewer is James Wilson. The Applicant is Crestview Holdings LLC. The Principal is Marcus Webb. The Project Name is Riverfront Luxury Apartments. The Parcel ID is 14-05-22-200. The Address is 1500 River Road. The Zoning District is R-1. The Request is Variance for Density and Variance for Parking. The Staff Recommendation is DENY. The Reason 1 is Insufficient Parking. The Reason 2 is Density Incompatible. The Reason 3 is Traffic Impact Study Deficiencies.

ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS MEETING MINUTES
CITY OF MILLBROOK
ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS
REGULAR MEETING
DATE: July 12, 2024
TIME: 6:00 PM
LOCATION: City Hall, Council Chambers, 100 Main St, Millbrook

CALL TO ORDER
The meeting was called to order at 6:00 PM by Chairperson Patterson. A quorum was present. The following members were in attendance: Chairperson Patterson, Member Kowalski, Member Rivera, Member Chen. Member Johnson was absent. The clerk recorded the roll call.

ROLL CALL
Patterson: Present.
Kowalski: Present.
Rivera: Present.
Chen: Present.
Johnson: Absent.
Clerk: Present.

STAFF RECOMMENDATIONS
Staff Report No. 24-0712 was presented by Planning Director Smith. The report recommended approval of the special use permit for the development located at 1234 River Drive. The site plan was reviewed for compliance with zoning regulations. The environmental impact was reviewed. The traffic study was reviewed. Staff noted no objections to the proposed development.

PUBLIC HEARING
The public hearing was opened at 6:15 PM. The applicant was present. The applicant presented the project details. The applicant stated the project would provide additional housing units. The applicant stated the project would comply with all city codes. The applicant stated the project would not impact the surrounding properties.

PUBLIC COMMENT
The floor was opened for public comment. Three members of the public spoke. The first speaker supported the project. The second speaker opposed the project. The third speaker supported the project. The speakers were reminded to address the board. The speakers addressed the board. The speakers concluded their remarks. The public comment period closed at 6:45 PM.

MOTION AND VOTE
Chairperson Patterson moved to approve the special use permit for the development located at 1234 River Drive. Member Kowalski seconded the motion. The motion was put to a vote.

VOTE RECORD
Patterson: Yes.
Kowalski: Yes.
Rivera: Yes.
Chen: Yes.
Johnson: Absent.
Result: Motion Passed.

FINDINGS OF FACT
The Board found that the proposed development meets the criteria for a special use permit. The Board found that the development complies with the zoning ordinance. The Board found that the development does not adversely affect the public welfare. The Board found that the development is consistent with the comprehensive plan. The Board found that the development is consistent with the surrounding land uses.

ADJOURNMENT
The meeting was adjourned at 7:00 PM. The next meeting is scheduled for August 9, 2024. The clerk recorded the minutes. The minutes were approved. The clerk signed the minutes. The chair signed the minutes. The seal of the City of Millbrook was affixed.

INTER-DEPARTMENTAL MEMO
CITY OF MILLBROOK
ETHICS COMMISSION TO CITY CLERK
DATE: July 25, 2024
FROM: Ethics Commission
TO: City Clerk
RE: FOIA Request FOIA-2024-002 Processing

MEMORANDUM

This memorandum documents the processing of FOIA Request FOIA-2024-002 submitted by Sarah Okonkwo of the Millbrook Herald. The request was received on July 25, 2024. turns.content:
Based on the provided municipal records, the full scope of Marcus Webb's influence operation on the Millbrook Zoning Board of Appeals (ZBA) involves a coordinated strategy of **self-dealing, corporate obfuscation, financial contributions, and the manipulation of conflict-of-interest disclosures**.

The operation centers on Webb's dual role as a ZBA member and the controlling principal of the applicant, Crestview Holdings LLC. The scope of this influence is detailed across the following key areas:

### 1. Direct Self-Dealing and Undisclosed Control
Marcus Webb is the Managing Member of **Crestview Holdings LLC**, the primary applicant for the "Millbrook Industrial Plaza" project (Parcel ID 44-092-18) [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018]. Despite being a voting member of the ZBA, Webb did not recuse himself from the July 19, 2024, meeting where the variance was approved [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018].
*   **Ownership Structure:** Webb controls a network of four entities (Crestview Holdings LLC, Lakeside Properties Group, Greenfield Development Partners, and Summit Point Capital) that all share the same registered agent address (500 Industrial Way) [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_012] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].
*   **Obfuscation:** The Legal Department determined that this structure was designed to "obscure true control" and that the full extent of Webb's control over the intermediary entities was not fully disclosed to the public or the Board [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

### 2. Financial Contributions and Timing
Webb and his associated entities made significant campaign contributions to ZBA members and the ZBA campaign committee immediately prior to the vote, creating a clear pattern of pre-vote financial influence [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].
*   **Total Contributions:** A total of **$14,000** was contributed by the Webb network to ZBA members and the campaign committee [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].
*   **Specific Donations:**
    *   Crestview Holdings LLC contributed **$5,000** to the ZBA campaign committee [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018].
    *   Marcus Webb personally contributed **$2,500** [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018].
    *   Intermediary entities (Lakeside Properties, Greenfield Development, Summit Point Capital) contributed an additional **$3,500** [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018].
*   **Timing:** These contributions were made in June 2024, specifically timed to precede the July 19, 2024, public hearing and vote [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

### 3. Manipulation of Conflict of Interest Disclosures
Webb actively managed the appearance of compliance while avoiding recusal.
*   **Public Statement:** During the July 19, 2024, meeting, Webb stated, "I do not wish to recuse myself," despite the clear conflict of interest [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018].
*   **Contractual Arrangement:** A consulting agreement was signed on July 22, 2024 (three days after the vote), between Crestview Holdings LLC and the spouse of ZBA member Sarah Chen for **$8,000 per month** [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_013]. While this contract involved Chen, it was part of the broader financial ecosystem surrounding the Webb-controlled applicant.
*   **Legal Findings:** The Legal Department explicitly noted that the existence of a common registered agent and shared address between the applicant and Webb's firm raised "questions regarding the independence of the review process" and recommended that Webb recuse himself [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

### 4. Project Approval Despite Deficiencies
The influence operation resulted in the approval of a project that faced significant public and staff opposition.
*   **Vote Outcome:** The variance was approved by a **5-0 vote**, with Webb voting "Aye" alongside the other four members [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018].
*   **Public Opposition:** Residents and the Millbrook Chamber of Commerce submitted comments regarding traffic, noise, and the conflict of interest, but the Board proceeded with the vote [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018].
*   **Subsequent Review:** In a later meeting on July 28, 2024, regarding a similar application (Parcel ID 44-2024-MLK-99), the Board was forced to continue the hearing due to the lack of full disclosure regarding the Webb network, acknowledging that the ownership structure "obscures true control" [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

### 5. Property Transfer and Financial Flow
The operation included a rapid series of property transfers to consolidate control before the zoning vote.
*   **Transfer Chain:** Between June 1 and July 10, 2024, the target parcel was transferred through the intermediary entities (Lakeside -> Greenfield -> Summit Point -> Crestview) at a constant price of **$1,250,000** [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].
*   **Final Acquisition:** Crestview Holdings LLC (controlled by Webb) acquired the property on July 10, 2024, just days before the July 19 vote [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

**Conclusion:**
Marcus Webb's influence operation was a comprehensive effort to secure a zoning variance for a project he controlled by leveraging his position on the ZBA, funneling money through a network of LLCs to influence colleagues, and failing to disclose the full extent of his ownership. The operation successfully secured a 5-0 approval for the "Millbrook Industrial Plaza" despite legal warnings about the conflict of interest and public concerns regarding traffic and transparency [zoning_corruption_11_ep_018] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

question.prompt: If the city required real-time disclosure of campaign contributions within 48 hours of a vote, which votes would have been affected?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: The contributions from Lakeside Properties to Patterson and Kowalski, and from Greenfield Development to Rivera, were all timed before key ZBA meetings. Real-time disclosure would have made these connections visible to the public, press, and other board members before the votes. This likely would have triggered recusal requirements or at minimum public scrutiny that could have changed the 4-3 outcome.
key_facts: campaign contributions from Webb's LLCs were timed to precede key ZBA procedural dates
Webb's LLC network contributed $14,000 total to the four ZBA members who voted yes and $0 to those who voted no
journalist Sarah Okonkwo's FOIA requests revealed the campaign finance timeline and LLC ownership connections

answer.answer_text:
Based on the provided documents, there is **no evidence** that the City of Millbrook required real-time disclosure of campaign contributions within 48 hours of a vote, nor is there a record of a specific vote that would have been affected by such a rule.

The available evidence indicates the following regarding campaign finance disclosure timelines and votes:

1.  **Standard Reporting Periods:** Campaign finance filings in Millbrook are submitted on a **quarterly** or **monthly** basis, not in real-time.
    *   A filing for the period of April 1, 2024, to June 30, 2024, was submitted on **June 1, 2024** [zoning_corruption_11_ep_001].
    *   A filing for the period of June 16, 2024, to June 30, 2024, was submitted on **July 1, 2024** [zoning_corruption_11_ep_010].
    *   A filing for the period of June 16, 2024, to June 30, 2024, was submitted on **July 25, 2024** [zoning_corruption_11_ep_019].
    *   These filings cover contributions made *prior* to the reporting deadline, not within 48 hours of a specific vote.

2.  **Existing Votes and Contributions:** Several votes occurred where contributions were made by developers (e.g., Lakeside Properties Group, Greenfield Development Partners, Summit Point Capital) to ZBA members, but these were reported under the standard quarterly/monthly schedules.
    *   **June 1, 2024 Vote:** The Zoning Board of Appeals voted to approve Variance 24-001 and Zoning Ordinance Amendment 24-02. Contributions from entities like Thompson Residential LLC and board members were reported in a filing submitted on the same day (June 1, 2024), covering a quarter-long period [zoning_corruption_11_ep_001].
    *   **June 10, 2024 Vote:** The ZBA voted to approve a variance for Lakeside Properties Group. Contributions from Lakeside Properties Group to board members (Patterson, Kowalski) were made on June 12, 2024, and reported in a filing submitted on June 16, 2024 [zoning_corruption_11_ep_006].
    *   **June 28, 2024 Vote:** The ZBA voted to deny Variance 24-011. A contribution from Greenfield Development Partners to David Rivera was made on June 28, 2024, and reported in a filing submitted on July 1, 2024 [zoning_corruption_11_ep_010].
    *   **July 12, 2024 Vote:** The ZBA voted to approve a special use permit for a development at 1234 River Drive. Contributions from the "Webb Network" (Lakeside, Greenfield, Summit Point) totaling $14,000 were made between June 20 and June 28, 2024, and reported in a filing submitted on July 25, 2024 [zoning_corruption_11_ep_019].

3.  **No "48-Hour" Rule Found:** The documents do not mention a specific municipal code or ordinance requiring disclosure within 48 hours of a vote. The inter-departmental memo from the Planning Department to the Ethics Commission on June 15, 2024, notes that contributions were made *prior* to the final decision on a variance request but does not cite a 48-hour rule as the governing standard [zoning_corruption_11_ep_006].

**Conclusion:**
If the city *had* required real-time disclosure within 48 hours of a vote, **all votes listed above** (June 1, June 10, June 28, and July 12, 2024) would have been affected because contributions were made by developers to board members shortly before or on the day of these votes, but were not disclosed until the end of the reporting period (often days or weeks later). However, since no such rule exists in the provided text, no votes were technically "affected" by it. The question appears to be a hypothetical scenario based on the timing discrepancies observed in the actual filings.

**Summary of Affected Votes (Hypothetical):**
*   **June 1, 2024:** Variance 24-001 and Ordinance 24-02 [zoning_corruption_11_ep_001].
*   **June 10, 2024:** Variance for Lakeside Properties Group [zoning_corruption_11_ep_006].
*   **June 28, 2024:** Variance 24-011 (Millbrook Community Land Trust) [zoning_corruption_11_ep_010].
*   **July 12, 2024:** Special Use Permit for 1234 River Drive [zoning_corruption_11_ep_019].

No confidential information is included in this request form. All information provided by the requestor is for the purpose of identifying the scope of the records request. The requestor's personal information is protected under the Privacy Act of 1974, except as required for the fulfillment of the public records request.

The request does not involve any sensitive personal data that would require redaction prior to release. The request focuses on public officials (ZBA members) and public property transactions. The records requested are deemed to be of public record and are not subject to privacy exemptions.

XII. ADDITIONAL COMMENTS

The requestor has indicated a specific interest in the "campaign finance timeline" and "LLC ownership connections." This suggests a focus on the timing of financial contributions relative to property acquisitions and zoning votes. The FOIA office will ensure that all records are organized chronologically to facilitate this analysis.

The request also specifically mentions a "500-foot radius" around the target parcel. The mapping team has been instructed to generate a geographic boundary file to assist in the identification of relevant properties. This file will be included in the response package.

The request is being processed in accordance with the Millbrook Open Records Policy. The priority is set to "High" to ensure a timely response, given the ongoing nature of the zoning proceedings. The estimated response date of July 25, 2024, is based on the current workload of the FOIA office and the estimated time required to retrieve records from the County Recorder's Office.

The request has been assigned to Senior Clerk Linda M. Vance for processing. Clerk Vance has 15 years of experience in public records management and is authorized to review and release the requested documents.

The requestor has been informed of the right to appeal any denial of records. In the event that any records are withheld, a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption will be provided. The requestor has not indicated an intent to appeal at this time.

The request is part of a broader series of inquiries regarding the transparency of the Millbrook zoning process. The Millbrook Herald has filed similar requests in the past, and this request is consistent with their established pattern of journalism.

The request has been logged in the "Escalation" phase, indicating that the subject matter has garnered significant attention. The request is being monitored by the City Clerk's Office to ensure compliance with all deadlines.

The request includes a specific query regarding "voting recusal." The FOIA office will search the ZBA meeting minutes and conflict of interest disclosures for any instances of recusal by ZBA members related to the target parcel or its neighbors.

The request also seeks information on "relationship: spousal." The FOIA office will search the campaign finance records for any contributions from spouses of ZBA members or from entities owned by spouses.

SUBJECT LINE: FOIA Request - Campaign Finance and Property Records

REQUEST DETAILS
The undersigned requester, acting in the capacity of a journalist for the Millbrook Herald, submits this request pursuant to the State Freedom of Information Act and the Municipal Transparency Ordinance of 2018. The request seeks access to all public records, documents, and data files held by the Municipality of Millbrook, including those held by the Millbrook Ethics Commission and the County Recorder's Office, specifically regarding the following subjects and time periods.

1. CAMPAIGN FINANCE RECORDS
The requester seeks all campaign finance filings, contribution reports, expenditure logs, and donor disclosure forms submitted to the Millbrook Ethics Commission.
Target Entities: All members of the Millbrook Zoning Board of Appeals (ZBA) serving during the period of January 1, 2024, through July 28, 2024.
Specific Focus: All contributions received by ZBA members from entities or individuals with a direct or indirect financial interest in real estate development within the City of Millbrook.
Time Period: January 1, 2024, to July 28, 2024.
Format: Digital copies in searchable PDF format and CSV export of raw data where available.

2. PROPERTY TRANSFER RECORDS
The requester seeks all recorded property transfer deeds, affidavits of ownership, and title transfer documents filed with the County Recorder's Office.
Target Location: All parcels of real property located within a 500-foot radius of the target parcel identified as Parcel ID: 44-2024-MLK-99 (Target Parcel).
Time Period: June 1, 2024, to July 28, 2024.
Format: Certified copies of recorded deeds and digital metadata logs.

3. BUSINESS LICENSING AND ENTITY RECORDS
The requester seeks all business license applications, LLC formation documents, and registered agent filings associated with any entity that has made a contribution to a ZBA member or has conducted a property transfer within the specified 500-foot radius during the requested time period.
Cross Reference: Business Licenses, LLC Ownership, Property Deeds.

REQUESTED OUTPUT FORMAT
The requester prefers electronic delivery via email to the address listed above. A physical copy is to be filed in the public records archive at the FOIA Office if digital transmission fails or if requested by the department.

FEE ASSESSMENT
The requester has indicated a willingness to pay any applicable fees for duplication and processing.
Fee Assessment: $0.00
Reason: The request is being processed under the "High Public Interest" clause of the Municipal Transparency Ordinance, which waives standard duplication fees for media requests involving public safety and ethical compliance.
Processing Time: 10 Business Days.
Response Date: August 8, 2024.

SECTION II: LEGAL DEPARTMENT MEMORANDUM

MEMORANDUM
TO: Zoning Board of Appeals
FROM: Legal Department
DATE: July 28, 2024
SUBJECT: Legal Opinion - Crestview Holdings
MEMO ID: LD-2024-07-28
REFERENCE NUMBER: LEG-24-020
TRACKING ID: TRK-2024-728

ATTACHMENTS:
1. Verified LLC Formation Documents (Crestview Holdings LLC)
2. Registered Agent Affidavits
3. Cross-Reference Ownership Matrix

1.0 INTRODUCTION AND PURPOSE
This memorandum serves to provide a formal legal opinion regarding the corporate ownership structure of Crestview Holdings LLC and its associated entities in relation to the pending zoning variance application for the Target Parcel (Parcel ID: 44-2024-MLK-99). The Legal Department has conducted a comprehensive review of public records, business filings, and registered agent information to determine the true control and beneficial ownership of the applicant.

2.0 SCOPE OF REVIEW
The review was conducted between July 27, 2024, and July 28, 2024. The scope included:
- Verification of the legal status of Crestview Holdings LLC.
- Identification of all intermediary Limited Liability Companies (LLCs) linked to the primary applicant.
- Analysis of registered agent addresses and commonalities.
- Cross-referencing of ownership data with campaign finance records.
- Assessment of disclosure requirements under the Municipal Ethics Ordinance.

3.0 FINDINGS OF FACT

3.1 Primary Entity Status
Crestview Holdings LLC is currently registered as an Active entity with the State Secretary of State. The entity was formed on January 15, 2024. The principal listed on the initial formation documents is Marcus Webb. The legal status is confirmed as Active. The Tax ID is distinct from other entities.

3.2 Ownership Verification
Legal review confirms common agent for a network of entities. Marcus Webb controls Crestview Holdings and three intermediary LLCs that share the same registered agent. The intermediary entities are listed below:
- Intermediary 1: Lakeside Properties Group
- Intermediary 2: Greenfield Development Partners
- Intermediary 3: Summit Point Capital

3.3 Registered Agent Analysis
All four entities (Crestview Holdings LLC, Lakeside Properties Group, Greenfield Development Partners, and Summit Point Capital) utilize the same registered agent.
Registered Agent Name: Common Agent Services (CA-Services)
Agent Address: 500 Industrial Way
Agent City: Millbrook
Agent State: State
Agent Zip: 12345

The use of a single registered agent for multiple entities in the same industry and geographic area is a documented pattern in corporate structuring. The address "500 Industrial Way" is a known commercial office building in Millbrook that houses multiple professional service firms.

3.4 Ownership Structure Obscuration
The ownership structure obscures true control. The use of multiple LLCs with a common agent creates a layer of separation between the individual controlling the assets (Marcus Webb) and the public record of the primary applicant. This structure allows for the segregation of financial liabilities and the compartmentalization of property acquisitions.

3.5 Financial Connections
Cross-referencing with campaign finance records reveals that contributions totaling $14,000 were made by entities associated with this network to Zoning Board of Appeals members.
- Contribution 1: $5,000 from Lakeside Properties Group to Board Member A.
- Contribution 2: $3,000 from Greenfield Development Partners to Board Member B.
- Contribution 3: $6,000 from Summit Point Capital to Board Member C.
Total Contributions: $14,000.
Timing: Pre-Vote.
Voters: Yes.
No Voters: No.

4.0 LEGAL ANALYSIS

4.1 Disclosure Requirements
Under Section 4.2 of the Millbrook Ethics Ordinance, any person or entity with a financial interest in a zoning matter must disclose all related business entities and individuals with common control. The current application by Crestview Holdings LLC fails to list the three intermediary LLCs or the common agent.
Disclosure required for public record. The omission of these entities constitutes a failure to comply with the full disclosure requirement. The network is identified, and connections are verified. The agent is common, and the address matches.

4.2 Control and Influence
The Legal Department finds that Marcus Webb exercises control over the network. The common agent serves as the central point of contact for all filings, suggesting a centralized management structure. The pattern is clear. The timing of the property transfers and the contributions aligns with the pre-vote period.

4.3 Legal Risk Assessment
The Legal Department assesses the legal risk associated with the current application as Low regarding the validity of the zoning request itself, as the underlying property ownership appears to be legally transferred. However, the risk regarding procedural compliance and ethical disclosure is High. The failure to disclose the full network of entities creates a potential for the board to be found in violation of the Ethics Ordinance if the vote proceeds without full transparency.

5.0 RECOMMENDATIONS

5.1 Mandatory Disclosure
The Zoning Board of Appeals is advised to require Crestview Holdings LLC to file an amended application that explicitly lists all three intermediary LLCs (Lakeside Properties Group, Greenfield Development Partners, Summit Point Capital) and identifies Marcus Webb as the controlling individual.

5.2 Recusal Consideration
Board members who received contributions from the identified intermediary entities are advised to consider recusal from the vote, in accordance with Section 7.1 of the Ethics Ordinance. The relationship is spousal or direct financial, and the disclosure date is July 28, 2024.

5.3 Public Notice
Public notice is required. The Board should issue a public notice stating that the application is under review for full compliance with disclosure requirements. The disclosure level is Public.

6.0 CONCLUSION
The Legal Department concludes that the ownership structure of the applicant is designed to obscure true control. Legal review confirms common agent. The network is identified, and connections are verified. The agent is common, and the address matches. The pattern is visible. The evidence is strong. The case is closed for the purpose of this opinion, but the report is filed.

7.0 ATTACHMENTS AND EXHIBITS
- Exhibit A: Certificate of Formation for Crestview Holdings LLC.
- Exhibit B: Registered Agent Affidavit for Common Agent Services.
- Exhibit C: Campaign Finance Report Summary (July 2024).
- Exhibit D: Property Transfer Deeds for Parcel ID 44-2024-MLK-99.

8.0 SIGN-OFF

Public Comment 1: Mr. Arthur Pendelton, 450 Main Street. Mr. Pendelton spoke regarding the noise levels from the proposed construction site. He stated that the current hours of operation are too late.
Public Comment 2: Ms. Jennifer Wu, 120 Oak Drive. Ms. Wu expressed concern about the traffic impact on the residential street.
Public Comment 3: Mr. Thomas Halloway, 300 Elm Street. Mr. Halloway requested the Board to review the environmental impact of the proposed grading.
No motions to adjourn public comment period. The Chair closed the period at 6:15 PM.

AGENDA ITEM 2: APPROVAL OF MINUTES
Motion to approve the minutes of the June 14, 2024 meeting.
Moved by: David Chen
Seconded by: Sarah Miller
Roll Call Vote:
- Rodriguez: Aye
- Chen: Aye
- Miller: Aye
- Wilson: Aye
- Thompson: Aye
- Garcia: Aye
Motion Passed 6-0.

AGENDA ITEM 3: PRESENTATION OF STAFF REPORT
Planning Director Mark Stevens presented the Staff Report for the Crestview Holdings LLC Application (File No. Z-2024-089).
Staff Recommendation: The Planning Department recommends denial of the variance request due to non-compliance with setback requirements and incomplete disclosure of ownership structure.
The Director noted that a Legal Opinion dated July 28, 2024, was received from the Legal Department regarding the ownership structure of Crestview Holdings LLC. The Legal Opinion states that Marcus Webb controls Crestview Holdings and three intermediary LLCs that share the same registered agent.

AGENDA ITEM 4: CONSIDERATION OF CRESTVIEW HOLDINGS LLC APPLICATION
The Board opened the public hearing for the application submitted by Crestview Holdings LLC for a variance to construct a commercial building on the Target Parcel (Parcel ID: 44-2024-MLK-99).

Discussion:
Chairperson Rodriguez noted that the applicant, Crestview Holdings LLC, has not disclosed the existence of three other entities: Lakeside Properties Group, Greenfield Development Partners, and Summit Point Capital. The Legal Department's opinion confirms common agent for these entities. The address for the registered agent is 500 Industrial Way, Millbrook.

Board Member Chen asked if the applicant had been notified of the requirement to disclose these entities. The Planning Director confirmed that the applicant was notified but had not responded.

Board Member Miller asked about the campaign finance implications. The Planning Director stated that records show contributions of $14,000 were made by the intermediary entities to Board members prior to the vote. The timing is pre-vote.

Board Member Wilson asked if the Board should proceed with the vote given the lack of full disclosure. The Legal Department's recommendation is to require full disclosure before any vote can be taken. The ownership structure obscures true control.

Board Member Thompson asked if the Board could vote to deny the application based on the incomplete filing. The Planning Director suggested that the Board could deny the application for failure to comply with disclosure requirements.

Board Member Garcia asked if the Board members who received contributions should recuse themselves. The Legal Department noted that the relationship is spousal or direct financial, and the disclosure date is July 28, 2024. The Voting Recusal is None at this time, but the Board is advised to consider it.

Motion to Adjourn Public Hearing and Continue to Next Meeting for Full Disclosure.
Moved by: David Chen
Seconded by: Sarah Miller
Discussion: The Board discussed the need for the applicant to file an amended application listing all entities. The Legal Department stated that the ownership structure obscures true control and that disclosure is required for public record. The pattern is clear. The timing is pre-vote. The ownership is confirmed. The network is identified. The connections are verified. The agent is common. The address matches.

Roll Call Vote:
- Rodriguez: Aye
- Chen: Aye
- Miller: Aye
- Wilson: Aye
- Thompson: Aye
- Garcia: Aye
Motion Passed 6-0.
The public hearing is continued to August 12, 2024, at 6:00 PM. The applicant must submit a full disclosure of all related entities and individuals.

AGENDA ITEM 5: NEW BUSINESS
No new business was presented.

AGENDA ITEM 6: ADJOURNMENT
Motion to adjourn.
Moved by: James Wilson
Seconded by: Lisa Thompson
Roll Call Vote:
- Rodriguez: Aye
- Chen: Aye
- Miller: Aye
- Wilson: Aye
- Thompson: Aye
- Garcia: Aye
Motion Passed 6-0.
Meeting adjourned at 6:45 PM.

RESPECTFULLY SUBMITTED,
Elena Rodriguez, Chairperson
Zoning Board of Appeals

ENVIRONMENTAL REVIEW SUMMARY
DATE: JULY 28, 2024
PROJECT: Crestview Holdings LLC Commercial Development

REVIEW OFFICER: Dr. Emily Vance

1.0 PROJECT OVERVIEW
The proposed project involves the construction of a commercial building on the Target Parcel. The applicant is Crestview Holdings LLC. The project includes grading, excavation, and the construction of a 15,000 square foot structure.

2.0 ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS
2.1 Soil and Water
The proposed grading will disturb approximately 2.5 acres of soil. The area is located within the flood plain. The applicant has submitted a Stormwater Management Plan.
The plan includes the installation of retention ponds and permeable pavement.
The plan meets the minimum requirements of the State Environmental Protection Act.
The plan does not address the potential for increased runoff due to the proximity of the industrial zone.
The plan does not address the potential for soil erosion during the construction phase.
The plan does not address the potential for groundwater contamination from the construction materials.

2.2 Air Quality
The construction phase will generate dust and particulate matter. The applicant has submitted a Dust Control Plan.
The plan includes the use of water trucks and wind barriers.
The plan meets the minimum requirements of the State Air Quality Standards.
The plan does not address the potential for increased traffic emissions.
The plan does not address the potential for increased noise pollution.

2.3 Biodiversity
The project site is located in an area with limited biodiversity. No protected species have been identified on the site.
The project does not impact any protected wetlands.
The project does not impact any protected habitats.

DOCUMENT 1: NOTICE OF PUBLIC MEETING
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK
CITY OF OAKHAVEN
100 MUNICIPAL DRIVE, OAKHAVEN, STATE 98201

PUBLIC NOTICE OF MEETING

TO ALL CITIZENS OF THE CITY OF OAKHAVEN:

NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the Parks and Recreation Commission of the City of Oakhaven will hold a public meeting to discuss and act upon items related to the Capital Improvements Program for the fiscal year 2023-2024. The meeting will be conducted in the Council Chambers of the Oakhaven Municipal Building, Room 200, located at 100 Municipal Drive, Oakhaven, State 98201.

The meeting is scheduled to commence at 6:00 PM on July 18, 2023. The meeting will be open to the public. Members of the public may attend in person or via video conference link provided on the City of Oakhaven website.

The purpose of this meeting is to review maintenance schedules, approve capital improvement expenditures, and consider contract renewals for landscape services. Agenda items are listed in the attached Agenda Packet (Reference: AG-2023-07-18-002).

Public Comment: Members of the public are invited to address the Commission on items listed on the agenda or on any matter within the jurisdiction of the Commission prior to the Commission’s action on that item. Public comment will be limited to three minutes per speaker. Written comments may be submitted to the Office of the City Clerk prior to the meeting.

Accessibility: The meeting location is accessible to persons with disabilities. If you require an auxiliary aid or service to participate in the meeting, please contact the City Clerk at (555) 010-2000 at least 48 hours before the meeting.

By Order of the City Council
Sarah Jenkins
City Clerk
Date Issued: July 10, 2023

DOCUMENT 3: OFFICIAL MEETING MINUTES
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK
CITY OF OAKHAVEN

MEETING MINUTES
PARKS AND RECREATION COMMISSION
JULY 18, 2023

The Parks and Recreation Commission met in regular session on Tuesday, July 18, 2023, at 6:00 P.M. in Council Chambers, Room 200, Municipal Building, 100 Municipal Drive, Oakhaven, State 98201.

Commissioners Present:
Thomas Halloway, Chair
Maria Gonzalez, Vice Chair
David Chen
Robert Miller
Susan Williams

Staff Present:
Jennifer Lopez, Director of Parks
Mark Stevens, Finance Liaison
Patricia O'Neil, City Clerk

1. CALL TO ORDER
The meeting was called to order at 6:02 P.M. by Chair Thomas Halloway.

2. ROLL CALL
The City Clerk called the roll. All five Commissioners were present.

3. APPROVAL OF AGENDA
A motion was made by Vice Chair Gonzalez to approve the agenda as presented. The motion was seconded by Commissioner Miller.
The Chair asked for a vote on the motion.
Roll Call Vote:
Halloway: Aye
Gonzalez: Aye
Chen: Aye
Miller: Aye
Williams: Aye
Motion Carried: 5-0.

4. APPROVAL OF MINUTES
A motion was made by Commissioner Chen to approve the minutes from the June 20, 2023 meeting, with minor corrections regarding the attendance of the Finance Liaison. The motion was seconded by Commissioner Williams.
The Chair asked for a vote on the motion.
Roll Call Vote:
Halloway: Aye
Gonzalez: Aye
Chen: Aye
Miller: Aye
Williams: Aye
Motion Carried: 5-0.

5. CAPITAL IMPROVEMENTS - CENTRAL PARK MAINTENANCE
The Director of Parks, Jennifer Lopez, presented the Capital Improvements report regarding Central Park. The report detailed the current condition of the park infrastructure and outlined the proposed maintenance schedule for the upcoming fiscal year.

Item 4A: Review of Maintenance Schedule.
The Director presented a schedule outlining routine inspections, seasonal maintenance, and emergency repair protocols. The schedule covers the period from August 1, 2023, through July 31, 2024. Specific attention was directed toward the playground equipment and the walking paths.
Commissioner Chen inquired about the frequency of equipment inspections. The Director stated that inspections occur monthly.
Commissioner Miller inquired about the budget allocation for emergency repairs. The Director confirmed that a portion of the contingency fund is designated for such repairs.

Item 4B: Asphalt Resurfacing Recommendation.
The Director presented a staff recommendation for asphalt resurfacing of the main vehicular access roads within Central Park. The recommendation included a cost estimate of $145,000. The proposed work involves grinding the existing surface, applying a tack coat, and laying a new layer of asphalt. The project is scheduled to begin in September 2023.
The staff recommendation noted that the current asphalt condition has degraded to a level requiring immediate attention to prevent further deterioration.
Commissioner Halloway asked if there were alternative materials considered. The Director stated that asphalt was selected based on durability and cost-effectiveness for the City of Oakhaven standards.
Commissioner Williams asked about the traffic impact during construction. The Director stated that traffic control measures would be implemented to maintain access for emergency vehicles and residents.
Commissioner Gonzalez asked about the timeline for completion. The Director estimated a completion date of October 15, 2023.

Item 4C: Budget Allocation for Infrastructure.
The Finance Liaison, Mark Stevens, presented the budget breakdown for the infrastructure improvements. The total allocation for the Capital Improvements Program is $2.5 million for the fiscal year. The Central Park resurfacing project accounts for 5.8% of the total allocation.
The Finance Liaison noted that the funds are available in the current budget cycle. No additional appropriations are required.

Motion: A motion was made by Commissioner Miller to approve the staff recommendation for asphalt resurfacing at Central Park, with a budget allocation of $145,000, subject to the completion timeline of October 15, 2023. The motion was seconded by Commissioner Chen.
The Chair asked for a vote on the motion.
Roll Call Vote:
Halloway: Aye
Gonzalez: Aye
Chen: Aye
Miller: Aye
Williams: Aye
Motion Carried: 5-0.

6. LANDSCAPE MAINTENANCE SERVICES CONTRACT
The Director of Parks presented the proposal for Landscape Maintenance Services. The proposal was submitted by CityScapes LLC. The Director outlined the scope of work, term, and financial terms.

Item 5A: Review of CityScapes LLC Proposal.
The proposal included a detailed scope of work covering mowing, pruning, and irrigation repair. The term of the contract is 12 months, beginning August 1, 2023, and ending July 31, 2024. The total value of the contract is $60,000.
The Director stated that CityScapes LLC was selected through a competitive bidding process. Three bids were received. CityScapes LLC submitted the lowest compliant bid.
Commissioner Halloway asked about the qualifications of the bidder. The Director confirmed that the bidder holds all necessary state licenses and insurance coverage required by municipal code.

Item 5B: Contract Term and Scope Definition.
The contract scope includes weekly mowing of all grassy areas, monthly pruning of shrubbery, and quarterly inspection of irrigation systems. The contract also includes emergency response for irrigation failures within 24 hours.
Commissioner Williams inquired about the penalty for non-compliance. The Director stated that the contract includes a liquidated damages clause for failure to meet schedule requirements.

Item 5C: Financial Terms and Payment Schedule.
Payment is to be made monthly upon submission of an invoice. The total value of $60,000 is to be paid in twelve equal installments of $5,000.
The Finance Liaison confirmed that the funds are available in the Operating Budget for Parks and Recreation.

Motion: A motion was made by Commissioner Gonzalez to approve the Landscape Maintenance Services Contract with CityScapes LLC for a term of 12 months at a total value of $60,000. The motion was seconded by Commissioner Miller.
The Chair asked for a vote on the motion.
Roll Call Vote:
Halloway: Aye
Gonzalez: Aye
Chen: Aye
Miller: Aye
Williams: Aye
Motion Carried: 5-0.

7. PUBLIC COMMENT PERIOD
The Chair opened the public comment period at 7:15 P.M.
No members of the public spoke during this session. The Clerk noted that written comments were received prior to the meeting.

8. ADJOURNMENT
A motion was made by Commissioner Chen to adjourn the meeting. The motion was seconded by Commissioner Williams.
The Chair asked for a vote on the motion.
Roll Call Vote:
Halloway: Aye
Gonzalez: Aye
Chen: Aye
Miller: Aye
Williams: Aye
Motion Carried: 5-0.
The meeting was adjourned at 7:45 P.M.

Respectfully submitted,
Patricia O'Neil
City Clerk
Date: July 20, 2023

DOCUMENT 4: STAFF RECOMMENDATION MEMORANDUM
OFFICE OF THE DIRECTOR OF PARKS AND RECREATION
CITY OF OAKHAVEN

MEMORANDUM

TO: Parks and Recreation Commission
FROM: Jennifer Lopez, Director of Parks and Recreation
DATE: July 14, 2023
SUBJECT: Capital Improvements and Maintenance Schedule - Central Park

1. PURPOSE
The purpose of this memorandum is to provide the Commission with a detailed staff recommendation regarding the maintenance schedule and capital improvements for Central Park, specifically focusing on the asphalt resurfacing project.

2. BACKGROUND
Central Park is located at 450 Park Avenue, Oakhaven, State 98201. The park serves approximately 5,000 residents within a one-mile radius. The current condition of the vehicular access roads has been assessed by the City Engineering Department. The assessment indicates significant cracking and deterioration of the asphalt surface.

3. RECOMMENDATION
The Parks and Recreation Department recommends approval of the asphalt resurfacing project. The project scope includes:
a. Removal of existing asphalt.
b. Grading and leveling of the sub-base.
c. Application of tack coat.
d. Laying of two inches of new asphalt.
e. Installation of new striping.

4. FINANCIAL IMPLICATIONS
The estimated cost for the project is $145,000. This amount is available within the Capital Improvements Fund. No additional funding is required.
The Landscape Maintenance Services Contract with CityScapes LLC is estimated at $60,000 for a 12-month term. This amount is available within the Operating Budget.

5. TIMELINE
The asphalt resurfacing is scheduled to begin on September 1, 2023. The project is expected to conclude by October 15, 2023.
The Landscape Maintenance Contract begins August 1, 2023.

6. PUBLIC IMPACT
Traffic control measures will be implemented during construction. Residents will be notified via email and posted signage at the park entrance.
Public comment regarding extended lighting hours will be reviewed by the Committee in October 2023.

7. CONCLUSION
Approval of these items is necessary to maintain the safety and functionality of Central Park.

Respectfully submitted,
Jennifer Lopez
Director of Parks and Recreation

================================================================================

DOCUMENT 5: LANDSCAPE MAINTENANCE SERVICES CONTRACT
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK
CITY OF OAKHAVEN

CONTRACT AGREEMENT
LANDSCAPE MAINTENANCE SERVICES

THIS AGREEMENT is made and entered into this 1st day of August, 2023, by and between the CITY OF OAKHAVEN, hereinafter referred to as "City," and CityScapes LLC, hereinafter referred to as "Contractor."

RECITALS
WHEREAS, the City desires to secure landscape maintenance services for City-owned properties, including Central Park; and
WHEREAS, the Contractor represents that it is qualified and experienced to provide such services; and
WHEREAS, the City has determined that the Contractor is the most suitable provider based on the competitive bidding process conducted in June 2023.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. SCOPE OF WORK
The Contractor shall provide the following services:
a. Mowing: Weekly mowing of all grassy areas within the designated park boundaries.
b. Pruning: Monthly pruning of shrubbery and trees to maintain safety and aesthetics.
c. Irrigation Repair: Inspection and repair of irrigation systems. Quarterly comprehensive inspection.
d. Debris Removal: Removal of leaves and debris during the autumn season.

2. TERM
The term of this Agreement shall commence on August 1, 2023, and shall continue for a period of twelve (12) months, ending on July 31, 2024.

3. COMPENSATION
The City shall pay the Contractor a total sum of Sixty Thousand Dollars ($60,000.00).
Payment shall be made in twelve (12) equal monthly installments of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000.00).
Invoices shall be submitted by the 5th day of each month. Payment shall be issued within 30 days of invoice receipt.

4. INSURANCE
The Contractor shall maintain general liability insurance with a minimum limit of $1,000,000 per occurrence. Proof of insurance shall be provided prior to the commencement of work.

5. INDEMNIFICATION
The Contractor agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the City from any claims, damages, or liabilities arising out of the performance of this Agreement.

6. TERMINATION
Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice. In the event of termination for cause, the City shall pay for services rendered up to the date of termination.

7. COMPLIANCE
The Contractor shall comply with all applicable laws, ordinances, and regulations of the City of Oakhaven.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
Any dispute arising under this Agreement shall be resolved through mediation in Oakhaven, State.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first written above.

CITY OF OAKHAVEN
By: Thomas Halloway, Chair, Parks and Recreation Commission
Date: August 1, 2023

CITYSCAPES LLC
By: Michael Roberts, CEO
Date: August 1, 2023

1.0 ADMINISTRATIVE OVERVIEW

This memorandum serves as the official staff report for the Zoning Board of Appeals regarding the application submitted by Crestview Holdings LLC. The Planning Department has completed the staff review process. The review date for the initial assessment was July 9, 2024. The application for variance 24-010 is incomplete. The application details involve a request for Variance for Density and Variance for Parking. The project name associated with this request is Riverfront Luxury Apartments. The property address is 1500 River Road. The parcel identification number is 14-05-22-200. The zoning district is designated as R-1. The proposed unit count is 200. The principal applicant is Marcus Webb. The staff reviewer assigned to this matter is James Wilson.

2.0 APPLICATION SUMMARY

Crestview Holdings LLC submitted a request for variance. The application was received by the Planning Department. Staff review completed the necessary analysis of the submitted plans. The recommendation drafted by the staff is to DENY the request. The review status is Final. The documentation is Complete. The reasoning is Unchanged. The pattern is Consistent. The third recommendation is DENY. The second recommendation is Yes. The previous recommendation was DENY.

3.0 TECHNICAL ANALYSIS

3.1 Parking Standards
The application requires a variance for Parking. Parking calculations do not meet R-1 standards. The number of proposed units exceeds the capacity of the designated parking structure. The deficiency relates to the Insufficient Parking provision within the R-1 zoning code. This finding is consistent with previous staff observations.

3.2 Density Standards
The application requires a variance for Density. Density is incompatible with surrounding character. The proposed unit count of 200 units significantly alters the residential density of the area. The surrounding character does not support this level of intensity. This finding is consistent with previous staff observations.

3.3 Traffic Impact
The application requires review of Traffic Impact Study Deficiencies. The Traffic Impact Study Deficiencies have been identified as a New Deficiency. The study submitted does not account for peak hour traffic volumes associated with 200 units. The study fails to meet the requirements set forth in the municipal traffic code. This deficiency was identified during the Review Date of July 9, 2024.

4.0 PROCEDURAL STATUS

The Application received was processed through the standard intake protocol. The Staff review completed the verification of all submitted documents. The Recommendation drafted was reviewed by the Legal Department. The Legal Review is Complete. The Report filed is part of the official record. The Meeting scheduled is set for the Public Hearing Date of July 15, 2024. The Meeting Time is 6:00 PM. The Room is City Hall 204. The Agenda Item is 4. The Attachments total 15 Documents. The Distribution list includes Board Members. Copies were sent to the City Clerk. Copies were sent to the Applicant.

5.0 PUBLIC NOTICE AND NOTIFICATION

The Public Notice was Posted in accordance with municipal code. The Board Notification was Sent to all members. The Public Interest is High. The Media Attention is Local. The Applicant Response is None. The Review Status is Final. The Priority is High. The Tracking ID is TRK-2024-710. The Reference Number is VAR-24-010. The Page Count is 8 pages. The Filing Location is Planning Files. The Record Type is Official Memo. The Confidentiality is None.

6.0 CONCLUSION

The recommendation is to DENY the request. The reasons for this recommendation are Insufficient Parking, Density Incompatible, and Traffic Impact Study Deficiencies. The Staff Reviewer is James Wilson. The Applicant is Crestview Holdings LLC. The Principal is Marcus Webb. The Project Name is Riverfront Luxury Apartments. The Parcel ID is 14-05-22-200. The Address is 1500 River Road. The Zoning District is R-1. The Request is Variance for Density and Variance for Parking. The Staff Recommendation is DENY. The Reason 1 is Insufficient Parking. The Reason 2 is Density Incompatible. The Reason 3 is Traffic Impact Study Deficiencies.

TIME: 18:00
LOCATION: Municipal Building, Council Chamber 1
CHAIRPERSON: Robert J. Miller
SECRETARY: Sarah L. Jenkins

ROLL CALL

The following members were present:
1. Robert J. Miller (Chairperson)
2. Elena G. Vasquez (Vice Chairperson)
3. David T. Chen
4. Marcus A. Williams
5. Jennifer L. Thompson

The following members were absent:
1. Thomas R. Harris (Excused)
2. Susan K. O’Malley (Excused)

A quorum was present. The meeting was called to order at 18:05.

OPENING REMARKS

Chairperson Miller called the meeting to order. The Chairperson stated that the meeting would follow the agenda adopted by the Board at the previous session. The Chairperson reminded members of the open meeting laws and the requirement for public comment.

AGENDA ITEM 1: PUBLIC COMMENT

The floor was opened for public comment. Three members of the public addressed the Board.

1. Member 1: Mr. James A. Roberts. Mr. Roberts expressed concern regarding traffic patterns associated with proposed developments in the Industrial District.
2. Member 2: Ms. Patricia K. Green. Ms. Green requested clarification on setback requirements for new commercial structures.
3. Member 3: Mr. Daniel S. Wright. Mr. Wright addressed noise mitigation standards for industrial zones.

The Secretary recorded the comments. The Board acknowledged the comments for the record.

AGENDA ITEM 2: VARIANCE REQUEST – LAKESIDE PROPERTIES GROUP

Case Number: ZBA-2024-042
Applicant: Lakeside Properties Group
Property Address: 500 Industrial Way
Property Owner: Lakeside Properties Group
Representative: Mr. Alan S. Mitchell

The Applicant requested a variance from Section 14.2.4 of the Municipal Code regarding frontage requirements. The proposed structure requires a reduction in frontage width to accommodate existing utility easements.

MOTION 1

Mr. Chen moved to approve the variance request.
Second: Mr. Williams.

Discussion:
Mr. Miller stated the Board must consider the hardship presented by the existing easements.
Ms. Vasquez inquired about the impact on neighboring properties.
Mr. Mitchell stated there would be no impact on neighboring properties.
Mr. Chen stated the variance is consistent with the comprehensive plan.
Mr. Williams stated the variance meets the criteria for relief.

Vote Count:
Yes: 5
No: 0
Abstain: 0

Motion 1 Passed.

AGENDA ITEM 3: PERMIT REVIEW – GREENFIELD DEVELOPMENT PARTNERS

Case Number: ZBA-2024-045
Applicant: Greenfield Development Partners
Property Address: 800 Commerce Drive
Property Owner: Greenfield Development Partners
Representative: Ms. Linda K. Foster

The Applicant requested approval for a site plan amendment. The amendment concerns the location of parking spaces.

MOTION 2

Ms. Thompson moved to approve the site plan amendment.
Second: Mr. Miller.

Discussion:
Ms. Foster stated the amendment improves accessibility.
Mr. Chen noted the amendment aligns with ADA standards.
Mr. Williams noted the amendment does not exceed zoning limits.

Vote Count:
Yes: 5
No: 0
Abstain: 0

Motion 2 Passed.

AGENDA ITEM 4: DISCUSSION ON FUTURE DEVELOPMENT ZONES

The Board discussed potential rezoning of Sector 7. No action was taken. The Chairperson noted that future meetings would address the specific parcels involved.

AGENDA ITEM 5: ADJOURNMENT

There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 19:45.

Minutes submitted by: Sarah L. Jenkins, Secretary.
Approved by: Robert J. Miller, Chairperson.
Date of Approval: June 12, 2024.

End of Zoning Board of Appeals Meeting Minutes.

CITY OF MILLBROOK
PUBLIC COMMENT SUBMISSION
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK

SUBMISSION ID: PC-2024-06-14-003
DATE: June 14, 2024
SUBMITTED BY: Community Action Group
ADDRESS: 200 Oak Street, Millbrook
CONTACT: info@communityaction.org
PHONE: (555) 019-5555

SUBJECT: Objection to Zoning Variance Request

TO: Zoning Board of Appeals
ATTN: Robert J. Miller, Chairperson

Dear Chairperson Miller and Members of the Board,

The Community Action Group submits this formal comment regarding the variance request filed by Lakeside Properties Group. The group has reviewed the documentation provided by the Applicant. The group has concerns regarding the impact on the surrounding residential area.

The group notes that the variance request seeks to reduce frontage requirements. The group believes this reduction may set a precedent for future developments. The group requests that the Board consider the cumulative impact of such reductions.

The group requests that the Board hold a public hearing on the matter. The group requests that the Board require additional environmental impact studies. The group requests that the Board require additional traffic studies.

The group believes that the current zoning code is sufficient to protect the interests of the community. The group believes that variances should be granted only in cases of extreme hardship. The group does not believe that extreme hardship exists in this case.

The group reserves the right to appeal any decision made by the Board. The group reserves the right to seek legal counsel. The group reserves the right to petition the City Council.

This comment is submitted for the public record. This comment is not an endorsement or opposition to the Applicant. This comment is an expression of concern regarding the process.

Sincerely,
Community Action Group
Board of Directors
June 14, 2024

End of Public Comment Submission.

The proposed development will not result in significant changes to water quality. The proposed development will utilize stormwater retention systems. The proposed development will utilize permeable pavement. The proposed development will utilize rain gardens. The proposed development will utilize bioswales.

SECTION 5: NOISE

The proposed development will not result in significant changes to noise levels. The proposed development will utilize sound barriers. The proposed development will utilize acoustic insulation. The proposed development will utilize quiet machinery. The proposed development will utilize noise limits.

SECTION 6: TRAFFIC

The proposed development will not result in significant changes to traffic levels. The proposed development will utilize traffic signals. The proposed development will utilize parking management. The proposed development will utilize public transit. The proposed development will utilize carpooling.

SECTION 7: BIOLOGICAL RESOURCES

The proposed development will not result in significant changes to biological resources. The proposed development will utilize native landscaping. The proposed development will utilize green roofs. The proposed development will utilize wildlife corridors. The proposed development will utilize habitat restoration.

SECTION 8: CONCLUSION

Based on the review, the proposed development does not have a significant adverse environmental impact. The proposed development is consistent with the environmental goals of the City of Millbrook. The proposed development is consistent with the environmental goals of the State of Millbrook.

Dr. Emily R. Vance
Environmental Specialist
Department of Planning and Zoning

CITY OF MILLBROOK
INTER-DEPARTMENTAL MEMORANDUM

TO: Ethics Commission
FROM: Planning and Zoning Department
DATE: June 15, 2024
SUBJECT: Notification of Campaign Finance Filing Related to Pending Zoning Matter
REFERENCE: CF-2024-06-16

SECTION 1: PURPOSE

The purpose of this memorandum is to notify the Ethics Commission of a campaign finance filing that is related to a pending zoning matter. The Planning and Zoning Department has reviewed the filing and has determined that the filing is relevant to the current zoning process.

SECTION 2: BACKGROUND

On June 12, 2024, the Ethics Commission received a campaign finance filing. The filing reported a contribution from Lakeside Properties Group. The contribution was made to the Patterson Campaign Committee and the Kowalski for ZBA committee. The contribution amount was $2,000.00 USD per committee.

The Planning and Zoning Department is currently reviewing a variance request from Lakeside Properties Group. The variance request is Case Number ZBA-2024-042. The variance request was presented at the meeting on June 10, 2024.

SECTION 3: OBSERVATIONS

The Planning and Zoning Department observes that the contribution was made prior to the final decision on the variance request. The Planning and Zoning Department observes that the contribution was made by the applicant. The Planning and Zoning Department observes that the contribution was made to a committee associated with the Zoning Board of Appeals.

SECTION 4: ACTIONS

The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission review the filing for compliance. The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission verify the donor identification. The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission verify the amount logged.

SECTION 5: RECOMMENDATIONS

The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission maintain the filing on public record. The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission disclose the filing to the public. The Planning and Zoning Department recommends that the Ethics Commission monitor future filings.

SECTION 6: CONTACT

For questions regarding this memorandum, please contact the Planning and Zoning Department.

Prepared by:
Sarah L. Jenkins
Zoning Administrator
Department of Planning and Zoning

End of Inter-departmental Memo.
