CITY OF MILLBROOK
ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS
REGULAR MEETING MINUTES

MEETING ID: ZBA-2024-06-28
DATE: June 28, 2024
LOCATION: City Hall Room 204
SUBJECT: Regular Meeting Minutes - June 28, 2024
AUTHOR: Zoning Board of Appeals
RECIPIENTS: City Clerk, Public Record

PROCEEDINGS OF THE REGULAR MEETING OF THE ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS OF THE CITY OF MILLBROOK HELD ON THE 28TH DAY OF JUNE, 2024, AT THE CITY HALL ROOM 204, 100 MAIN ST, MILLBROOK.

CALL TO ORDER
The meeting was called to order at 6:00 PM by Chairperson David Rivera. The Chairperson noted that the meeting was convened pursuant to the authority granted under Chapter 14 of the Millbrook Municipal Code. The Chairperson announced the availability of the Webcast Available: Yes for the public viewing via the City of Millbrook official website portal.

ATTENDANCE AND ROLL CALL
The following members of the Zoning Board of Appeals were present:
Member Marcus Patterson
Member Elena Kowalski
Member David Rivera
Member Sarah Chen
Alternate Member: None

City Staff Present:
ZBA Clerk: Present
Administrative Assistant: Recorder
Legal Counsel: Present
Planning Department Representative: Present

The Chairperson declared that a Quorum Established was present.

ROLL CALL OF MEMBERS
The Secretary called the roll.
Member Marcus Patterson: Present
Member Elena Kowalski: Present
Member David Rivera: Present
Member Sarah Chen: Present
Member: Absent
Member: Absent

The Chairperson confirmed the presence of four (4) voting members, constituting a quorum. The Recorder noted the Audio File: Recorded and Video File: Recorded for the proceedings. The Recorder noted that the Transcript: Pending.

CONSENT AGENDA
The Board considered the Consent Agenda. No items were presented for discussion or objection. The motion to approve the Consent Agenda was made and seconded. The vote was unanimous.

REGULAR ORDER OF BUSINESS

ITEM 1: VARIANCE REQUEST 24-011
APPLICANT: Millbrook Community Land Trust
PROPERTY ADDRESS: 1600 Community Drive
REQUEST TYPE: Setback Variance
ZONING DISTRICT: R-1

The Board considered the Land Trust application. The Staff Report ID: PD-2024-06-20 was reviewed by the Board. The Staff Recommendation: APPROVE was read into the record by the Planning Department Representative. The representative outlined the technical specifications of the proposed project.

PROJECT DETAILS
The project is classified as Project Type: Affordable Housing. The proposal includes a Unit Count: 40 residential units. The property is located within the R-1 Zoning District. The Federal Grant ID: FHA-2024-99 was cited in the application documentation as the funding source for the development.

PUBLIC COMMENT
The Chairperson opened the Public Comment section. The Public Comment Log: Attached was referenced. A total of Public Speakers: 15 individuals registered to speak. The following list represents the speakers who addressed the Board during this session:

1.  James Henderson, 1605 Community Drive, Support

The comments submitted by the public were noted in the official log. The Community Reaction: Strong Opposition was recorded in the staff summary based on the volume and nature of the comments received. The Media Coverage: Local News was noted as a factor in the public awareness of this hearing.

STAFF PRESENTATION AND TECHNICAL REVIEW
The Planning Department Representative presented the Staff Report ID: PD-2024-06-20. The report detailed the zoning compliance issues associated with the proposed construction. The Board considered the Land Trust application regarding the variance request.

During the technical review, the Board reviewed the setback calculations submitted with the application. It was noted that the Setback calculation was found to be non-compliant. The technicality cited was specifically regarding the Setback Calculation required by Section 5-102 of the Municipal Code. The Applicant Response: Pending was recorded regarding the request for clarification on the specific measurements.

MEMBER DISCUSSION
Member Marcus Patterson raised the issue of the setback requirements. Member Elena Kowalski inquired about the grant implications. Member David Rivera facilitated the discussion. Member Sarah Chen noted the zoning district requirements.

The Board discussed the implications of the variance request on the surrounding properties. The Grant Impact: Contingency at Risk was discussed as part of the staff briefing regarding the Federal Grant ID: FHA-2024-99. The Board acknowledged the Project Type: Affordable Housing designation.

MOTION AND SECOND
The Board moved to the vote on the variance request. Member Marcus Patterson made a motion. The Motion to deny was made by Member Patterson. Member Elena Kowalski seconded the motion. The Seconded by Member Kowalski was recorded in the minutes.

The discussion focused on the technical compliance of the setback variance. The Chairperson called for a vote on the motion.

VOTE RECORD
The Chairperson called for a roll call vote.
Member Marcus Patterson: No
Member Elena Kowalski: No
Member David Rivera: No
Member Sarah Chen: Yes

The Vote was 4-3 to deny. The Roll Call Vote: Yes (3) was recorded. The Roll Call Vote: No (4) was recorded. The Roll Call Vote: Abstain (0) was recorded.

The Motion Carried. The denial of the variance request 24-011 was officially recorded.

ORDER OF DENIAL

MEMORANDUM
This memorandum serves as formal notification of the decision rendered by the Zoning Board of Appeals during the Regular Meeting held on June 28, 2024. The decision pertains to Variance Request 24-011 submitted by Millbrook Community Land Trust.

ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS MEETING MINUTES
CITY OF MILLBROOK
ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS
REGULAR MEETING
DATE: July 12, 2024
TIME: 6:00 PM
LOCATION: City Hall, Council Chambers, 100 Main St, Millbrook

CALL TO ORDER
The meeting was called to order at 6:00 PM by Chairperson Patterson. A quorum was present. The following members were in attendance: Chairperson Patterson, Member Kowalski, Member Rivera, Member Chen. Member Johnson was absent. The clerk recorded the roll call.

ROLL CALL
Patterson: Present.
Kowalski: Present.
Rivera: Present.
Chen: Present.
Johnson: Absent.
Clerk: Present.

STAFF RECOMMENDATIONS
Staff Report No. 24-0712 was presented by Planning Director Smith. The report recommended approval of the special use permit for the development located at 1234 River Drive. The site plan was reviewed for compliance with zoning regulations. The environmental impact was reviewed. The traffic study was reviewed. Staff noted no objections to the proposed development.

PUBLIC HEARING
The public hearing was opened at 6:15 PM. The applicant was present. The applicant presented the project details. The applicant stated the project would provide additional housing units. The applicant stated the project would comply with all city codes. The applicant stated the project would not impact the surrounding properties.

PUBLIC COMMENT
The floor was opened for public comment. Three members of the public spoke. The first speaker supported the project. The second speaker opposed the project. The third speaker supported the project. The speakers were reminded to address the board. The speakers addressed the board. The speakers concluded their remarks. The public comment period closed at 6:45 PM.

MOTION AND VOTE
Chairperson Patterson moved to approve the special use permit for the development located at 1234 River Drive. Member Kowalski seconded the motion. The motion was put to a vote.

VOTE RECORD
Patterson: Yes.
Kowalski: Yes.
Rivera: Yes.
Chen: Yes.
Johnson: Absent.
Result: Motion Passed.

FINDINGS OF FACT
The Board found that the proposed development meets the criteria for a special use permit. The Board found that the development complies with the zoning ordinance. The Board found that the development does not adversely affect the public welfare. The Board found that the development is consistent with the comprehensive plan. The Board found that the development is consistent with the surrounding land uses.

ADJOURNMENT
The meeting was adjourned at 7:00 PM. The next meeting is scheduled for August 9, 2024. The clerk recorded the minutes. The minutes were approved. The clerk signed the minutes. The chair signed the minutes. The seal of the City of Millbrook was affixed.

{"ref_id": "zoning_corruption_11_ep_005", "text": "CITY OF OAKHAVEN
ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS
REGULAR MEETING MINUTES

**MEETING ID:** ZBA-2024-06-13
**DATE:** June 13, 2024
**LOCATION:** City Hall, Room 204, 100 Municipal Drive, Oakhaven, CA 90210
**TIME:** 6:00 PM – 7:15 PM
**SUBJECT:** Regular Meeting Minutes - June 13, 2024

**I. CALL TO ORDER AND ROLL CALL**

The Zoning Board of Appeals (hereinafter referred to as "the Board") of the City of Oakhaven convened in regular session on the 13th day of June, 2024, at 6:00 PM. The meeting was held in Room 204 of City Hall, pursuant to the provisions of the Municipal Code and the City's administrative procedures for public hearings and quasi-judicial proceedings. The meeting was webcast via the City's official digital platform, and a recording was made for archival purposes.

Board members present at the commencement of the meeting were:
1.  Robert Thompson, Chair
2.  Linda Martinez, Vice-Chair
3.  James Wilson, Member
4.  Sarah Chen, Member
5.  David Rivera, Member
6.  Elena Kowalski, Member

Absent: None.

The Chair, Robert Thompson, called the meeting to order at 6:00 PM. A roll call was taken to establish a quorum. Each member present affirmed their attendance and eligibility to vote. The Chair declared that a quorum was established at 6:02 PM, comprising six (6) of the seven (7) authorized members, satisfying the requirements of Section 17.4.2 of the Oakhaven Municipal Code.

The Chair announced the availability of the meeting agenda, which had been posted at the City Clerk's office and on the municipal website for a period of ten (10) days prior to the meeting, in compliance with the Brown Act and local open meeting laws. The agenda included the review of variance request 24-005, the approval of the minutes from the previous meeting held on May 30, 2024, and the scheduling of future hearings.

**II. APPROVAL OF MINUTES**

The Board proceeded to consider the minutes from the previous regular meeting held on May 30, 2024. The minutes had been distributed to all board members via electronic mail on June 5, 2024, and were made available for public review at the City Clerk's office.

No corrections, amendments, or additions were offered by any board member or member of the public during the review period.

Upon motion by Member Sarah Chen, seconded by Member James Wilson, the Board voted to approve the minutes of the May 30, 2024, meeting as submitted.

**Roll Call Vote on Approval of Minutes:**
*   **Robert Thompson:** Yes
*   **Linda Martinez:** Yes
*   **James Wilson:** Yes
*   **Sarah Chen:** Yes
*   **David Rivera:** Yes
*   **Elena Kowalski:** Yes

**Result:** Motion Carried (6-0).

The minutes were officially entered into the record.

**III. STAFF REPORTS AND PRESENTATIONS**

The Board received the regular staff report from the Office of Planning and Zoning. Staff Director, Marcus Patterson, was present to present the findings regarding the pending variance request. Mr. Patterson is the primary case manager assigned to the file and has been responsible for the preliminary review of the application materials.

Mr. Patterson reviewed the administrative file for Variance Request 24-005. The file included the initial application submitted by the applicant, Green Valley Farms, dated April 2, 2024. The file contained the completed application forms, the required filing fee of $450.00, and a comprehensive set of supporting documentation.

Mr. Patterson noted that the property in question is located at 890 Pine Lane, within the R-1 (Single Family Residential) zoning district. The subject property is a standard residential lot with a frontage of 60 feet and a depth of 120 feet. The request pertains to a proposed fence structure along the rear and side property lines.

The Staff Report detailed the specific parameters of the request. The applicant, Green Valley Farms, is seeking a variance to the height restriction provisions of the Oakhaven Municipal Code, specifically Section 15.2.4, which mandates a maximum fence height of 6 feet in the rear yard and 4 feet in the front yard for residential properties. The applicant is requesting permission to construct a fence with a maximum height of 8 feet.

The Staff Report indicated that the primary justification provided by the applicant for this variance is the need for enhanced privacy and security. The applicant stated that the property is situated adjacent to a busy thoroughfare and that the existing 6-foot fence does not provide sufficient visual screening from the road or adequate security for the livestock and equipment stored on the premises.

Mr. Patterson presented the findings of the site analysis. He reviewed the site plan attached to the application, which was prepared by a licensed civil engineer and bears the seal of the firm of Henderson & Associates. The site plan clearly delineated the property lines, the proposed location of the fence, and the dimensions of the structure. The plan indicated that the 8-foot fence would be constructed along the entire rear property line and the southern side property line, utilizing a solid wood construction with a privacy slat design.

Regarding the impact on neighboring properties, Mr. Patterson highlighted the correspondence on file. The neighboring property at 892 Pine Lane, owned by a private individual, had provided written consent to the proposed variance. A copy of this consent letter, dated May 15, 2024, was included in the administrative file. The letter explicitly stated that the neighbor had no objection to the increased fence height and acknowledged that the structure would not obstruct their view or light.

Mr. Patterson also reviewed the photo documentation submitted with the application. The file contained 12 digital images taken on April 1, 2024, depicting the current state of the property, the existing fencing, and the surrounding neighborhood context. These images showed a typical suburban residential street with similar lot sizes and setbacks. The photos confirmed that the proposed location for the fence would not encroach upon any public right-of-way or easements.

The Staff Report concluded with a formal recommendation. After a thorough review of the application, the site plan, the neighbor consent, and the public interest, the Planning Department staff recommended that the Board approve Variance Request 24-005. The staff noted that the request met the criteria for a variance as outlined in the Municipal Code, specifically that the variance would not alter the essential character of the neighborhood and that there was no adverse impact on surrounding properties.

Mr. Patterson stated, "The Board considered the request for variance 24-005. Staff noted no adverse impact on surrounding properties. The applicant has provided sufficient evidence to support the need for the variance, and the neighbor consent removes any potential conflict. Therefore, the staff recommendation is to approve the application."

The Chair thanked Mr. Patterson for the comprehensive report and the clear presentation of the facts. The Board members were given an opportunity to ask clarifying questions regarding the staff report.

**IV. PUBLIC COMMENT**

The Chair opened the floor for public comment regarding the items on the agenda. The Chair reminded the public that comments should be directed to the Board and that each speaker was limited to three (3) minutes.

The Chair called for public comment specifically regarding Variance Request 24-005.

The Clerk confirmed that no members of the public had signed up to speak prior to the meeting. During the public comment period, no members of the public requested the floor.

**Public Speakers:** 0

The Chair noted that the lack of public comment was consistent with the notification process, which had included a notice posted on the property and a letter sent to all abutting property owners. The Chair stated that the silence of the public did not preclude the Board from proceeding with the hearing, as the matter was fully briefed and the staff recommendation was favorable.

**V. CONSIDERATION OF VARIANCE REQUEST 24-005**

The Board proceeded to the consideration of Variance Request 24-005. The Chair called the item up for discussion.

**A. Motion and Second**

Member Linda Martinez, Vice-Chair, moved to approve Variance Request 24-005 for the property located at 890 Pine Lane, requesting a fence height variance of 8 feet in lieu of the 6-foot ordinance limit. The motion was based on the staff recommendation, the applicant's justification of privacy and security, and the consent of the neighboring property owner at 892 Pine Lane.

Member David Rivera seconded the motion.

**B. Discussion**

The Board engaged in a brief discussion regarding the motion.

Member Sarah Chen inquired about the materials proposed for the fence. Mr. Patterson responded that the site plan indicated the use of pressure-treated pine with a cedar stain, which is a standard material in the district and consistent with the aesthetic guidelines of the neighborhood.

Member Elena Kowalski asked if the fence would include any gates or driveways. Mr. Patterson confirmed that the design included a single access gate on the southern side, which would be 10 feet wide, and that the gate would operate within the property lines.

Member James Wilson raised a question regarding the drainage implications of the fence. Mr. Patterson noted that the site plan showed the fence would be installed with a 6-inch gap at the bottom to allow for surface water runoff, and that the engineer had confirmed this would not impede drainage patterns.

Member Robert Thompson, the Chair, reiterated the importance of the neighbor consent. He noted that the written consent from the property at 892 Pine Lane was a significant factor in the staff's recommendation and helped to mitigate any potential concerns regarding property boundaries or light obstruction.

**C. Voting Procedure**

The Chair called for a vote on the motion made by Member Martinez and seconded by Member Rivera. The vote was taken by roll call, as required by the Municipal Code for quasi-judicial proceedings.

**Roll Call Vote:**

1.  **Robert Thompson (Chair):** Yes
2.  **Linda Martinez (Vice-Chair):** Yes
3.  **James Wilson (Member):** Yes
4.  **Sarah Chen (Member):** Yes
5.  **David Rivera (Member):** Yes
6.  **Elena Kowalski (Member):** Yes

**Vote Summary:**
*   **Yes:** 6
*   **No:** 0
*   **Abstain:** 0

**D. Result**

The Chair declared the motion carried. The Board voted unanimously 6-0 to approve Variance Request 24-005.

The Chair stated, "Motion to approve was made by Member Martinez. Seconded by Member Rivera. Vote was unanimous 6-0."

The Board found that the request met the necessary criteria for a variance. The Board determined that the requested height of 8 feet was reasonable given the specific circumstances of the property, including the need for privacy and security, and that the construction would not adversely affect the health, safety, or welfare of the surrounding neighborhood. The Board further found that the neighbor consent provided by the owner of 892 Pine Lane confirmed that the variance would not cause hardship or nuisance to adjacent properties.

**VI. ORDER OF APPROVAL**

Pursuant to the vote, the Board directed the City Clerk to issue an Order of Approval for Variance Request 24-005. The Order was to be signed by the Chair and the City Clerk on the same day as the meeting.

**Order Details:**
*   **Order Number:** ORD-2024-13
*   **Date of Signing:** June 13, 2024
*   **Property Address:** 890 Pine Lane
*   **Applicant:** Green Valley Farms
*   **Variance Request:** 24-005
*   **Approval:** Fence Height Variance (8 feet)
*   **Distribution:** Applicant, City Clerk, Planning Department

The Order was prepared by the City Clerk's office and signed by Chair Robert Thompson and City Clerk Linda Martinez at 7:10 PM. The Order was immediately filed with the City Clerk's office and made available for public inspection. A copy of the Order was mailed to the applicant, Green Valley Farms, via first-class mail.

**VII. ADJOURNMENT**

The Chair called for a motion to adjourn the meeting.

Member Elena Kowalski moved to adjourn the meeting.

Member David Rivera seconded the motion.

The motion was approved by unanimous consent.

**Meeting End:** 7:15 PM

The Chair declared the meeting adjourned at 7:15 PM. The next regular meeting of the Zoning Board of Appeals is scheduled for June 27, 2024, at 6:00 PM in City Hall Room 204. The meeting was recorded and will be archived in the City Clerk's office.

**VIII. CERTIFICATION**

I, the undersigned, hereby certify that the foregoing is a true and correct copy of the minutes of the regular meeting of the Zoning Board of Appeals of the City of Oakhaven held on June 13, 2024. The minutes were adopted at the conclusion of the meeting and are hereby entered into the official records of the Board.

**Date:** June 13, 2024

**Linda Martinez**
City Clerk
Zoning Board of Appeals

____________________________________
**Robert Thompson**
Chair, Zoning Board of Appeals

The existing ordinance limits the height to 6 feet. The applicant states that the additional 2 feet is necessary to provide adequate privacy from the adjacent thoroughfare and to secure the livestock and equipment stored on the property.

**SECTION 4: SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION**

The following documents were submitted with this application:

**SECTION 6: STAFF REVIEW AND RECOMMENDATION**

**Staff Reviewer:** Marcus Patterson, Senior Planner
**Review Date:** June 5, 2024
**Staff Recommendation:** APPROVE

**Staff Findings:**
1.  **Hardship:** The applicant has demonstrated a specific hardship related to privacy and security that is unique to the property due to its location adjacent to a high-traffic area.
2.  **No Adverse Impact:** The variance will not alter the essential character of the neighborhood. The proposed height is consistent with the scale of the surrounding structures.
3.  **Neighbor Consent:** Consent has been obtained from the neighboring property at 892 Pine Lane, mitigating any potential conflicts.
4.  **Compliance:** The proposed fence design complies with all other zoning regulations, including setbacks and material restrictions.

**SECTION 7: BOARD ACTION**

**Date of Decision:** June 13, 2024
**Board Action:** Approved
**Vote Count:** 6-0 (Unanimous)
**Order Number:** ORD-2024-13

1.  The fence must be constructed in accordance with the site plan submitted with this application.
2.  The fence must not encroach upon any public right-of-way or easement.
3.  The fence must be maintained in good repair by the property owner.
4.  The variance applies only to the specific property at 890 Pine Lane and does not set a precedent for other properties.

Any party aggrieved by this decision may appeal to the City Council within 15 days of the date of this decision. The appeal must be filed in writing with the City Clerk.

**CITY OF OAKHAVEN**
**PUBLIC COMMENT SUBMISSION**
**SUBMISSION ID:** PC-2024-06-13-001
**DATE SUBMITTED:** June 13, 2024
**TIME:** 5:45 PM
**SUBMISSION METHOD:** Electronic Form (City Website)
**STATUS:** Received

**COMPLAINANT INFORMATION**

**Name:** John Doe
**Address:** 888 Pine Lane, Oakhaven, CA 90210
**Email:** j.doe@oakhaven.com
**Phone:** (555) 555-0100
**Relationship to Property:** Abutting Neighbor

**SUBMISSION DETAILS**

**Subject:** Public Comment on Variance Request 24-005
**Property Address:** 890 Pine Lane
**Hearing Date:** June 13, 2024

**Comment Text:**
I am writing to express my concern regarding the proposed variance for the property at 890 Pine Lane. While I understand the applicant's need for privacy, I believe that an 8-foot fence may still present issues regarding wind flow and drainage in the rear yard area. I have observed that the existing 6-foot fence on my property has caused some water pooling during heavy rains, and I am concerned that increasing the height of the fence on the adjacent property may exacerbate this issue.

Furthermore, I am concerned that the 8-foot fence may block the view of the street from my residence, which could be a safety concern for pedestrians and vehicles. I request that the Board consider the potential impact on the microclimate of the neighborhood and the safety of the public right-of-way.

I am not opposed to the concept of a fence, but I urge the Board to ensure that the design includes adequate drainage solutions and that the height is not excessive. I would appreciate it if the Board could review the drainage plan submitted by the applicant to ensure that no adverse effects will occur.

I am willing to attend the meeting to discuss this matter further if required.

**SECTION: RESPONSE FROM STAFF**

**Response Date:** June 13, 2024
**Respondent:** Marcus Patterson, Senior Planner
**Response Text:**
The staff has reviewed the public comment submitted by John Doe. The site plan submitted with the variance application includes a drainage analysis which indicates that the proposed fence will not impede water flow. The engineer has confirmed that the 6-inch gap at the bottom of the fence will allow for adequate drainage. Regarding the view obstruction, the staff notes that the variance is for a rear yard fence, which does not typically impact the view of the street from the front yard. The staff recommends that the Board proceed with the approval as the technical details have been addressed in the application.

***

**CITY OF OAKHAVEN**
**ENVIRONMENTAL REVIEW SUMMARY**
**PROJECT:** Variance Request 24-005 (Fence Height)
**LOCATION:** 890 Pine Lane, Oakhaven, CA 90210
**DATE:** June 13, 2024
**REVIEWER:** Environmental Compliance Office
**STATUS:** Exempt from CEQA Review

**SECTION 1: PROJECT DESCRIPTION**

The proposed project involves the construction of an 8-foot tall wooden fence along the rear and southern side property lines of the residential property located at 890 Pine Lane. The fence will be constructed of pressure-treated pine with a cedar stain. The project is classified as a minor land use action within the R-1 zoning district.

**SECTION 2: CEQA DETERMINATION**

Pursuant to the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA), the City of Oakhaven has determined that the proposed project is exempt from environmental review. The project falls under the following categorical exemption:
*   **Exemption Category:** Class 1 (Existing Facilities)
*   **Exemption Criteria:** The project involves the construction of a fence on an existing residential lot. The project does not involve any significant expansion of the lot, nor does it involve the construction of any new structures that would alter the physical environment.

**SECTION 3: IMPACT ANALYSIS**

**Air Quality:** No impact. The construction of a wooden fence does not involve heavy machinery or emissions-generating activities.
**Water Quality:** No impact. The project does not involve any work in or near water bodies. The drainage plan confirms that surface water runoff will not be impeded.
**Biological Resources:** No impact. The project is located within an urban residential area. No sensitive species or habitats are present on the property.
**Noise:** No impact. Construction noise will be temporary and limited to standard daylight hours.
**Traffic:** No impact. The project does not generate additional traffic.
**Land Use:** No impact. The project is consistent with the zoning regulations and the general plan.

**SECTION 4: CONCLUSION**

Based on the review, the Environmental Compliance Office concludes that the proposed project will not have a significant effect on the environment. The project is exempt from further environmental review.

**CITY OF OAKHAVEN**
**INTER-DEPARTMENTAL MEMO**

**TO:** Zoning Board of Appeals
**FROM:** Office of the City Manager
**DATE:** June 13, 2024
**SUBJECT:** Administrative Support for Variance Request 24-005

**MEMORANDUM**

This memorandum serves to document the administrative support provided by various city departments in relation to the variance request 24-005 for the property at 890 Pine Lane.

**1. PLANNING DEPARTMENT**
The Planning Department, led by Marcus Patterson, conducted the initial review of the application. They verified the completeness of the application, reviewed the site plan, and coordinated with the applicant to obtain the necessary neighbor consent. The department prepared the staff report and presented the findings to the Board.

**2. CITY CLERK'S OFFICE**
The City Clerk's Office was responsible for posting the notice of the meeting, maintaining the public record, and issuing the Order of Approval. The office also managed the webcast of the meeting and ensured that the minutes were accurately recorded.

**3. PUBLIC WORKS DEPARTMENT**
The Public Works Department reviewed the drainage plan submitted by the applicant. They confirmed that the proposed fence design would not impede surface water drainage and that the 6-inch gap at the bottom of the fence would allow for adequate runoff.

**4. FIRE DEPARTMENT**
The Fire Department reviewed the site plan to ensure that the proposed fence would not obstruct fire access or emergency vehicle movement. They confirmed that the fence design complied with all fire safety regulations.

**5. POLICE DEPARTMENT**
The Police Department was consulted regarding the security justification for the variance. They confirmed that the proposed fence height would not create a safety hazard and would not impede police visibility.

**6. FINANCE DEPARTMENT**
The Finance Department processed the application fee of $450.00 and the transfer tax of $850.00 associated with the property transfer. They verified that all payments were received and recorded.

**7. LEGAL DEPARTMENT**
The Legal Department reviewed the variance request and the staff report to ensure that the Board's decision was consistent with the Municipal Code and state law. They confirmed that the Board's action was within its authority.

**CONCLUSION**
All departments have provided the necessary support to ensure that the variance request 24-005 was processed in a timely and efficient manner. The Board's decision to approve the request was made in accordance with all applicable laws and regulations.

DOCUMENT 1: BOARD MINUTES - SPECIAL SESSION (MILLBROOK)

CITY OF CITY CENTER
ZONING BOARD OF APPEALS
SPECIAL SESSION MINUTES

DATE: July 19, 2024
TIME: 6:00 PM TO 8:00 PM
LOCATION: City Hall Room 204
MEETING ID: ZBA-07-19
RECORDER: Administrative Assistant

CALL TO ORDER
The Special Session was called to order by Chairperson Marcus Patterson at 6:00 PM. A quorum was established with the presence of seven (7) members. The following members were present and accounted for: Marcus Patterson, Elena Kowalski, David Rivera, Sarah Chen, Thompson, Martinez, and Wilson. The Zoning Board of Appeals convened for the purpose of hearing applications regarding zoning variances and land use requests.

AGENDA ITEM 1: APPLICATION 24-011
Applicant: Millbrook Community Land Trust
Property Address: 1600 Community Drive
Request Type: Setback Variance
Zoning District: R-1
Variance Request: 24-011

The Board considered the Land Trust application. Staff Report ID: PD-2024-06-20 was reviewed by the Board members. Staff Recommendation: APPROVE was noted in the official file. The application seeks a variance regarding the minimum setback requirements for the proposed affordable housing project.

PUBLIC COMMENT
The Board opened the floor for public comment. Public Speakers: 15 individuals registered to speak. Comments were recorded in the Public Comment Log attached to these minutes. Speakers included residents of the immediate vicinity, representatives of the Millbrook Community Land Trust, and members of the City Planning Department.

STAFF PRESENTATION
The Planning Department representative presented the technical findings regarding the variance request. The staff report highlighted the need for the variance to accommodate the design of the affordable housing units. The staff noted that the current zoning district R-1 allows for residential development but specifies strict setback lines.

BOARD DISCUSSION
Members discussed the implications of the variance. Discussion focused on the technical specifications of the property lines. The Board considered the technicality cited regarding the boundary alignment.

STATEMENT OF TECHNICALITY
Setback calculation was found to be non-compliant with the current code interpretation. The Board reviewed the survey data submitted by the applicant. The data indicated a discrepancy in the measured distance from the property line to the proposed structure.

MOTION AND VOTE
Motion to deny was made by Member Patterson. Seconded by Member Kowalski. The motion was placed before the Board for voting.

ROLL CALL VOTE
The Clerk called the roll for the vote on Motion 24-011-Deny.
Roll Call Vote: Yes (3)
Roll Call Vote: No (4)
Roll Call Vote: Abstain (0)

The Yes Voters were identified as Thompson, Martinez, Wilson. The No Voters were identified as Patterson, Kowalski, Rivera, Chen. The vote was 4-3 to deny. The Motion Carried.

ORDER ISSUED
Order Number: ORD-2024-19
Order Signed: July 19, 2024
Distribution: Applicant, City Clerk

The Board issued an Order denying the variance request. The denial was based on the finding that the setback calculation did not meet the minimum requirements of the Municipal Code. The Order states that the applicant may submit a revised application or appeal the decision.

APPEAL PROCESS
Appeal Deadline: July 19, 2025
Applicant Response: Pending
The Millbrook Community Land Trust was notified of the Appeal Deadline. The Applicant Response is currently Pending as of the time of this recording.

PROJECT DETAILS
Project Type: Affordable Housing
Unit Count: 40
Federal Grant ID: FHA-2024-99
Grant Impact: Contingency at Risk
Federal Grant: Contingent

The project is designated as Affordable Housing with a Unit Count of 40 units. A Federal Grant ID: FHA-2024-99 is associated with the funding for this development. The Grant Impact is noted as Contingency at Risk due to the variance denial. The Federal Grant status is Contingent upon the resolution of the zoning variance.

COMMUNITY AND MEDIA
Community Reaction: Strong Opposition
Media Coverage: Local News

The Community Reaction is recorded as Strong Opposition to the variance. Media Coverage: Local News reported on the proceedings of the meeting. The local news outlet covered the vote count and the specific details of the denial.

NEXT STEPS
Next Meeting: July 22, 2024
Location: City Hall Room 204
Contact: ZBA Clerk

The Board adjourned the session at 8:00 PM. The minutes were recorded and are subject to approval at the next meeting.

turns.content:
Based on the provided documents, there is **no evidence** that the City of Millbrook required real-time disclosure of campaign contributions within 48 hours of a vote, nor is there a record of a specific vote that would have been affected by such a rule.

The available evidence indicates the following regarding campaign finance disclosure timelines and votes:

1.  **Standard Reporting Periods:** Campaign finance filings in Millbrook are submitted on a **quarterly** or **monthly** basis, not in real-time.
    *   A filing for the period of April 1, 2024, to June 30, 2024, was submitted on **June 1, 2024** [zoning_corruption_11_ep_001].
    *   A filing for the period of June 16, 2024, to June 30, 2024, was submitted on **July 1, 2024** [zoning_corruption_11_ep_010].
    *   A filing for the period of June 16, 2024, to June 30, 2024, was submitted on **July 25, 2024** [zoning_corruption_11_ep_019].
    *   These filings cover contributions made *prior* to the reporting deadline, not within 48 hours of a specific vote.

2.  **Existing Votes and Contributions:** Several votes occurred where contributions were made by developers (e.g., Lakeside Properties Group, Greenfield Development Partners, Summit Point Capital) to ZBA members, but these were reported under the standard quarterly/monthly schedules.
    *   **June 1, 2024 Vote:** The Zoning Board of Appeals voted to approve Variance 24-001 and Zoning Ordinance Amendment 24-02. Contributions from entities like Thompson Residential LLC and board members were reported in a filing submitted on the same day (June 1, 2024), covering a quarter-long period [zoning_corruption_11_ep_001].
    *   **June 10, 2024 Vote:** The ZBA voted to approve a variance for Lakeside Properties Group. Contributions from Lakeside Properties Group to board members (Patterson, Kowalski) were made on June 12, 2024, and reported in a filing submitted on June 16, 2024 [zoning_corruption_11_ep_006].
    *   **June 28, 2024 Vote:** The ZBA voted to deny Variance 24-011. A contribution from Greenfield Development Partners to David Rivera was made on June 28, 2024, and reported in a filing submitted on July 1, 2024 [zoning_corruption_11_ep_010].
    *   **July 12, 2024 Vote:** The ZBA voted to approve a special use permit for a development at 1234 River Drive. Contributions from the "Webb Network" (Lakeside, Greenfield, Summit Point) totaling $14,000 were made between June 20 and June 28, 2024, and reported in a filing submitted on July 25, 2024 [zoning_corruption_11_ep_019].

3.  **No "48-Hour" Rule Found:** The documents do not mention a specific municipal code or ordinance requiring disclosure within 48 hours of a vote. The inter-departmental memo from the Planning Department to the Ethics Commission on June 15, 2024, notes that contributions were made *prior* to the final decision on a variance request but does not cite a 48-hour rule as the governing standard [zoning_corruption_11_ep_006].

**Conclusion:**
If the city *had* required real-time disclosure within 48 hours of a vote, **all votes listed above** (June 1, June 10, June 28, and July 12, 2024) would have been affected because contributions were made by developers to board members shortly before or on the day of these votes, but were not disclosed until the end of the reporting period (often days or weeks later). However, since no such rule exists in the provided text, no votes were technically "affected" by it. The question appears to be a hypothetical scenario based on the timing discrepancies observed in the actual filings.

**Summary of Affected Votes (Hypothetical):**
*   **June 1, 2024:** Variance 24-001 and Ordinance 24-02 [zoning_corruption_11_ep_001].
*   **June 10, 2024:** Variance for Lakeside Properties Group [zoning_corruption_11_ep_006].
*   **June 28, 2024:** Variance 24-011 (Millbrook Community Land Trust) [zoning_corruption_11_ep_010].
*   **July 12, 2024:** Special Use Permit for 1234 River Drive [zoning_corruption_11_ep_019].

question.prompt: What governance reforms would prevent this type of influence in the future?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: Require real-time campaign contribution disclosure (48-hour window before votes). Mandate beneficial ownership disclosure for all LLC applicants. Require automatic recusal when a board member or family member has a financial relationship with an applicant. Implement mandatory cooling-off periods between contributions and votes. Create an independent ethics review for variance approvals that override staff recommendations. Publish all property transfers within a radius of pending applications.
key_facts: Marcus Webb controls Crestview Holdings and three intermediary LLCs (Lakeside Properties Group, Greenfield Development Partners, Summit Point Capital) that share the same registered agent
campaign contributions from Webb's LLCs were timed to precede key ZBA procedural dates
ZBA member Chen's spouse was paid $8,000/month by Crestview as a consultant with the contract ending the week after the favorable vote
ZBA staff recommended DENY on the Crestview variance twice citing insufficient parking and traffic impact deficiencies

answer.answer_text:
Based on the provided evidence, the following governance reforms would prevent the type of influence (specifically the use of LLC networks to obscure ownership, the timing of campaign contributions prior to zoning votes, and the lack of full disclosure) observed in the Millbrook zoning corruption case:

**1. Mandatory "Beneficial Ownership" Disclosure for Zoning Applications**
The evidence shows that the applicant, Crestview Holdings LLC, obscured the true control of the network by using three intermediary LLCs (Lakeside Properties Group, Greenfield Development Partners, and Summit Point Capital) that shared a common registered agent but were not disclosed [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].
*   **Reform:** The Millbrook Ethics Ordinance (specifically Section 4.2) currently requires disclosure of related entities, but the evidence suggests this was insufficient to catch the "obscured true control" [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020]. A reform would require applicants to file a **Beneficial Ownership Statement** at the time of application, mandating the identification of *all* individuals with a controlling interest (direct or indirect) and *all* affiliated entities within a specific network, regardless of whether they are separate legal entities. This would prevent the "layer of separation" created by the common agent [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

**2. Mandatory Cooling-Off Periods for Campaign Contributions**
The evidence reveals a clear pattern where contributions totaling $14,000 were made by the intermediary entities to ZBA members (David Chen, Sarah Miller, James Wilson) specifically in the weeks leading up to the vote [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020]. The timeline analysis notes the contributions were "Pre-Vote" and the pattern was "Clear" [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].
*   **Reform:** Implement a **mandatory cooling-off period** (e.g., 6 to 12 months) during which any campaign contributions from entities with a direct financial interest in pending zoning matters are prohibited. Alternatively, contributions from such entities could be capped at a significantly lower limit or required to be reported immediately upon receipt, with a mandatory public disclosure window before any vote can be scheduled [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

**3. Automated Cross-Referencing of Property Transfers and Campaign Finance**
The investigation relied on a journalist (Sarah Okonkwo) manually cross-referencing property transfers within a 500-foot radius with campaign finance records to uncover the network [zoning_corruption_11_ep_015] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].
*   **Reform:** Establish an **automated public database** that links property transfer records (deeds) with campaign finance filings in real-time. The system should automatically flag any ZBA member who receives a contribution from an entity that has purchased property within a specific radius (e.g., 500 feet) of a pending zoning application within a set timeframe (e.g., 6 months) [zoning_corruption_11_ep_015] [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020]. This would remove the reliance on manual FOIA requests to detect the "Pre-Vote" timing and "LLC Ownership" connections [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

**4. Stricter Recusal Protocols and "Look-Through" Voting Rules**
The evidence indicates that while the Legal Department recommended recusal for members who received contributions, the Board initially proceeded without a formal recusal, noting the relationship was "spousal or direct financial" but the "Voting Recusal: None" status was recorded until the investigation was complete [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].
*   **Reform:** Enact a **"Look-Through" voting rule** where any ZBA member who receives a contribution from *any* entity in a network connected to an applicant (even if the contribution was made by an intermediary LLC) is automatically disqualified from voting on that matter. The current system allowed members to vote despite the "Clear" pattern of contributions [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020]. Additionally, the Ethics Commission should have the authority to **suspend votes** immediately upon the filing of a conflict disclosure, rather than waiting for a full investigation [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

**5. Enhanced Public Notice Requirements for "Shell" Entities**
The use of multiple LLCs with a common agent created a "network" that was not visible to the public until the FOIA request [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].
*   **Reform:** Require **enhanced public notice** for any zoning application submitted by an LLC that has a registered agent address shared with other entities in the same industry or geographic area. The notice must explicitly state the "Common Agent" and list all associated entities, forcing the public and the Board to scrutinize the network before the hearing begins [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

**6. Independent Ethics Review Prior to Scheduling**
The evidence shows that the Ethics Review was "Pending" even as the meeting proceeded, and the Legal Department's opinion was only issued on the day of the meeting (July 28, 2024) [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].
*   **Reform:** Mandate a **pre-hearing ethics certification** where the Ethics Commission must review and certify the full ownership network and contribution history of an applicant *before* the ZBA can schedule a public hearing. This would prevent the "Pre-Vote" contributions from influencing the process, as the vote could not be scheduled until the "Full Disclosure" was verified [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020].

These reforms address the specific failures identified in the Millbrook case: the use of LLCs to hide control [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020], the timing of contributions [zoning_corruption_11_ep_020], and the lack of automated detection [zoning_corruption_11_ep_015].

arguments.query: campaign contribution disclosure 48-hour requirement reform

{"ref_id": "zoning_corruption_11_ep_015", "text": "MILLBROOK ETHICS COMMISSION
FOIA OFFICE
100 MAIN STREET
MILLBROOK, COUNTY, STATE 55000

OFFICIAL RECORD OF REQUEST
DOCUMENT ID: REC-2024-150
REQUEST ID: FOIA-2024-001
TRACKING ID: TRK-2024-713
DATE OF FILING: July 13, 2024
TIME OF FILING: 09:15:00 EST

I. REQUESTER INFORMATION

NAME: Sarah Okonkwo
AFFILIATION: Millbrook Herald
ADDRESS: 450 Media Plaza, Suite 200, Millbrook
CONTACT NUMBER: (555) 012-9988
EMAIL ADDRESS: sokonkwo@millbrookherald.com
FAX NUMBER: (555) 012-9989

II. REQUEST SPECIFICATIONS

SUBJECT: FOIA Request - Campaign Finance and Property Records
CATEGORY: Public Records / Financial Disclosure / Real Estate Transactions
DATE RANGE OF RECORDS: June 1, 2024, to July 13, 2024
TARGET ENTITIES:
1. All members of the Millbrook Zoning Board of Appeals (ZBA).
2. All property transfers occurring within a 500-foot radius of the target parcel located at 1506 River Road.
3. Limited liability company (LLC) entities identified as owners or beneficial owners of said parcels.
4. Campaign finance contributions received by ZBA members during the specified period.

REQUESTED DOCUMENTS:
1. Complete campaign finance disclosure forms (Form ZBA-CF-2024) for all current ZBA members.
2. All contribution logs, donor lists, and expenditure reports filed with the Millbrook Ethics Commission between June 1, 2024, and July 13, 2024.
3. Deeds, titles, and transfer records for all properties located within a 500-foot radius of 1506 River Road.
4. Property tax assessment records for the identified parcels.
5. Business license records for any LLCs or corporate entities holding title to the target parcels.
6. Any correspondence between the County Recorder's Office and the Millbrook Ethics Commission regarding the aforementioned properties.
7. Disclosure statements filed by ZBA members regarding potential conflicts of interest.

III. REQUEST LOGISTICS AND FEES

PROCESSING COST ESTIMATE: $0.00
REASON FOR WAIVER: The request is made by a recognized news organization for the purpose of disseminating information to the general public. Under State Statute 12.4.1 (b)(3), no fees are to be assessed for requests made by media outlets regarding matters of significant public interest, provided the request does not require excessive duplication or retrieval costs exceeding $50.00.
DUPLICATION FEE: $0.00 (Digital delivery requested)
SEARCH TIME ESTIMATE: 4.5 hours (Estimated)
RETRIEVAL TIME ESTIMATE: 2.0 hours (Estimated)
TOTAL FEES: $0.00

IV. PROCESSING STATUS AND SCHEDULE

INITIAL STATUS: Pending
DATE SUBMITTED: July 13, 2024
DATE OF ACKNOWLEDGMENT: July 13, 2024
ACKNOWLEDGMENT NUMBER: ACK-2024-713
RESPONSE DEADLINE: July 25, 2024
ESTIMATED RESPONSE DATE: July 25, 2024
PRIORITY LEVEL: High
PUBLIC INTEREST DESIGNATION: High
LEGAL REVIEW STATUS: None
COMPLIANCE CHECK: Passed
DATA INTEGRITY CHECK: Verified
CONFLICT CHECK: None
ETHICS REVIEW: Pending

V. ADMINISTRATIVE NOTES

This request has been logged into the Millbrook Public Records Management System (MPRMS) under the "Escalation" phase of the current fiscal year's transparency initiative. The request targets specific zoning activities and financial disclosures related to the River Road corridor. The requestor has indicated an intent to publish findings in the Millbrook Herald.

The request is categorized as "High Priority" due to the proximity of the requested property records to a pending zoning variance application currently under review by the Zoning Board of Appeals. The request for campaign finance records is linked to the target entities' potential involvement in the aforementioned zoning proceedings.

The Millbrook Ethics Commission has been notified of the request. A preliminary review of the records suggests a high volume of data regarding property transfers in the specified radius. The County Recorder's Office has been contacted to facilitate the retrieval of deed records.

The request includes a specific inquiry into "LLC ownership connections." This requires cross-referencing business license data with property tax rolls. The FOIA Clerk has initiated a query against the State Business Registry database.

VI. RESPONSE FORMAT

The requested records will be provided in digital format (PDF) via secure email transmission to the address listed above. A physical copy will be filed in Room 101 of the FOIA Office for public inspection. The digital copy will be available for download via the Millbrook Public Records Portal within 24 hours of the final response generation.

The response will include:
1. A cover letter detailing the scope of the search and the documents produced.
2. Redacted copies of any documents where exemptions under State Statute 12.4.1 (c)(2) regarding personal privacy might apply (though none are anticipated for campaign finance and public property records).
3. A statement regarding the completeness of the search.
4. Contact information for the FOIA Clerk for any follow-up inquiries.

VII. VERIFICATION AND AUTHENTICATION

This document serves as the official record of the submission of the FOIA request. The digital signature of the submitting party, Sarah Okonkwo, has been verified via the Millbrook Secure Portal. The document has been timestamped and assigned a unique Record ID: REC-2024-150.

The authenticity of this request form is verified by the FOIA Clerk. The request is compliant with all local, state, and federal open records laws. No legal hold has been placed on the records requested, and no litigation hold is currently active that would impede the production of these documents.

The requestor has agreed to the terms of service regarding the use of the provided data. The data is provided for informational and journalistic purposes. Redistribution of the data is permitted in accordance with the Millbrook Herald's publication standards and public domain laws.

VIII. CONTACT INFORMATION

FOIA Clerk: James P. Henderson
Office: FOIA Office, 100 Main St, Millbrook
Phone: (555) 019-2025
Email: foia@millbrook.gov
Hours of Operation: Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM EST

For urgent inquiries regarding the status of this request, please reference Tracking ID: TRK-2024-713.

IX. ARCHIVE STATUS

The original signed copy of this request form has been scanned and stored in the digital archive. The physical copy has been filed in the "2024 July" folder. The file size of the digital document is 12 KB. The document is indexed under the keywords: "Campaign Finance," "Property Transfer," "ZBA," "River Road," "Sarah Okonkwo," "Millbrook Herald."

The archive status is marked as "Complete" regarding the initial intake. The file will remain open for the duration of the processing period and will be closed upon the final delivery of the requested records. The audit trail is digital and includes timestamps for all access and modification events.

X. CROSS-REFERENCE DATA

This request is cross-referenced with the following active files:
1. ZBA Case File: ZBA-2024-089 (Variance Application for 1506 River Road)
2. Campaign Finance File: CF-2024-045 (General Election Cycle)
3. Property Assessment File: PA-2024-112 (River Road Corridor)

The data integrity check has passed. The cross-reference with business licenses has been initiated. The consistency check has passed. The verification method is public records. The relationship status of the requestor to the target entities is "None." The disclosure date is July 13, 2024. The media coverage is designated as "Local." The public response is currently "None." The official response is "Pending."

XI. CONFIDENTIALITY AND PRIVACY

No confidential information is included in this request form. All information provided by the requestor is for the purpose of identifying the scope of the records request. The requestor's personal information is protected under the Privacy Act of 1974, except as required for the fulfillment of the public records request.

The request does not involve any sensitive personal data that would require redaction prior to release. The request focuses on public officials (ZBA members) and public property transactions. The records requested are deemed to be of public record and are not subject to privacy exemptions.

XII. ADDITIONAL COMMENTS

The requestor has indicated a specific interest in the "campaign finance timeline" and "LLC ownership connections." This suggests a focus on the timing of financial contributions relative to property acquisitions and zoning votes. The FOIA office will ensure that all records are organized chronologically to facilitate this analysis.

The request also specifically mentions a "500-foot radius" around the target parcel. The mapping team has been instructed to generate a geographic boundary file to assist in the identification of relevant properties. This file will be included in the response package.

The request is being processed in accordance with the Millbrook Open Records Policy. The priority is set to "High" to ensure a timely response, given the ongoing nature of the zoning proceedings. OFFICE OF THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES

SUBJECT: CONTRACT RATIFICATION AND MEETING RECORDS
DATE OF RECORD: 2023-10-12
CLASSIFICATION: PUBLIC RECORD
FORMAT: ELECTRONIC AND PAPER ARCHIVE

TRANSMITTAL MEMORANDUM

TO: DISTRICT ARCHIVIST, RECORDS MANAGEMENT DIVISION
FROM: SECRETARY OF THE BOARD, NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT
DATE: 2023-10-13
SUBJECT: SUBMISSION OF BUNDLE SB-DIST-002 REGARDING TEACHER UNION RATIFICATION

PURSUANT TO THE NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT OPEN MEETINGS ACT AND THE DISTRICT RECORDS RETENTION POLICY, THE FOLLOWING DOCUMENTS ARE HEREBY SUBMITTED FOR PERMANENT RETENTION. THIS BUNDLE CONTAINS THE OFFICIAL MEETING MINUTES FROM THE REGULAR BOARD MEETING HELD ON OCTOBER 12, 2023, AND THE EXECUTED CONTRACT AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE DISTRICT AND THE NORTHWOOD EDUCATION ASSOCIATION.

ALL DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN REVIEWED FOR COMPLETENESS IN ACCORDANCE WITH SECTION 4.1 OF THE DISTRICT ADMINISTRATIVE CODE. THE DOCUMENTATION INCLUDED IN THIS BUNDLE REFLECTS THE OFFICIAL VOTE RESULT OF 4-1 IN FAVOR OF RATIFICATION. THE CONTRACT EFFECTIVE DATE IS SET FOR JULY 1, 2024, FOR A TERM OF THREE YEARS.

PLEASE PROCESS THIS BUNDLE FOR FILING UNDER THE FINANCIAL AND HUMAN RESOURCES DIVISION.

SIGNED:
J. HARRIS
SECRETARY OF THE BOARD
NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT

NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT
BOARD OF TRUSTEES REGULAR MEETING AGENDA
DATE: OCTOBER 12, 2023
TIME: 6:00 P.M.
LOCATION: DISTRICT ADMINISTRATION BUILDING, ROOM 101, NORTHWOOD, TEXAS

CALL TO ORDER

1.0  PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE
    The meeting shall commence with the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag of the United States of America.

2.0  ROLL CALL
    The Secretary shall call the roll of the Board of Trustees to determine a quorum.

3.0  APPROVAL OF AGENDA
    Motion to approve the agenda as distributed.
    Motion: To approve the agenda.
    Second: By Trustee.

4.0  APPROVAL OF PREVIOUS MEETING MINUTES
    Approval of minutes from the meeting held on September 14, 2023.
    Motion: To approve the minutes as presented.
    Second: By Trustee.

5.0  PUBLIC COMMENT PERIOD
    General public comment on items not on the agenda.
    Duration: 15 minutes maximum.
    Protocol: Speakers must sign in at the registration desk and limit remarks to three minutes per speaker.

6.0  SUPERINTENDENT REPORT
    Superintendent R. Wilson shall provide a report on district operations, enrollment trends, and budget status.

7.0  CONSENT AGENDA
    Items to be considered by a single motion without separate discussion.
    7.1 Approval of vendor invoices for maintenance services.
    7.2 Approval of transportation route changes for elementary zones.
    7.3 Approval of new faculty employment contracts for the 2023-2024 academic year.

8.0  NEW BUSINESS
    8.1 Discussion and Vote on Teacher Union Contract Ratification.
        Presentation by Legal Counsel regarding the Collective Bargaining Agreement.
        Presentation by Union Representative S. Martinez regarding proposed terms.
        Presentation by Superintendent R. Wilson regarding budget impact.
        Public Comment on Item 8.1.
        Motion to Ratify.
        Vote on Motion.

9.0  PUBLIC COMMENT PERIOD (SPECIAL)
    Dedicated comment period regarding Item 8.1.
    Duration: 45 minutes.
    Protocol: Speakers must sign in at the registration desk. Speakers are limited to five minutes per speaker.

10.0 ADJOURNMENT
    Motion to adjourn the meeting.
    Second: By Trustee.

NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT
BOARD OF TRUSTEES REGULAR MEETING MINUTES
DATE: OCTOBER 12, 2023
TIME: 6:00 P.M.
LOCATION: DISTRICT ADMINISTRATION BUILDING, ROOM 101, NORTHWOOD, TEXAS

PRESIDING OFFICER: PRESIDENT M. THOMPSON
SECRETARY: J. HARRIS

CALL TO ORDER

The meeting was called to order at 6:00 P.M. by President M. Thompson.

ROLL CALL

Trustee M. Thompson was present.
Trustee L. Garcia was present.
Trustee K. O’Neill was present.
Trustee D. Chen was present.
Trustee A. Johnson was present.
Superintendent R. Wilson was present.
Legal Counsel J. Miller was present.
Union Representative S. Martinez was present.

A quorum was present.

PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE

The meeting commenced with the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag of the United States of America, led by Superintendent R. Wilson.

APPROVAL OF AGENDA

Motion to approve the agenda was made by Trustee L. Garcia and seconded by Trustee K. O’Neill.
The roll call vote on the agenda approval was as follows:
Trustee M. Thompson: Aye
Trustee L. Garcia: Aye
Trustee K. O’Neill: Aye
Trustee D. Chen: Aye
Trustee A. Johnson: Aye

The agenda was approved unanimously.

APPROVAL OF PREVIOUS MEETING MINUTES

Minutes from the meeting held on September 14, 2023, were reviewed by the Board.
Motion to approve the minutes was made by Trustee D. Chen and seconded by Trustee A. Johnson.
The roll call vote on the approval of minutes was as follows:
Trustee M. Thompson: Aye
Trustee L. Garcia: Aye
Trustee K. O’Neill: Aye
Trustee D. Chen: Aye
Trustee A. Johnson: Aye

The minutes were approved.

SUPERINTENDENT REPORT

Superintendent R. Wilson presented the district report. The report included enrollment figures for the current fiscal year, facility usage statistics, and the current status of the capital improvement plan. The report noted that enrollment has stabilized at 12,500 students. Facility usage is at 94% capacity during peak hours. The capital improvement plan is on schedule for the completion of the Northwood High School roof replacement project scheduled for the spring of 2024.

CONSENT AGENDA

Item 7.1: Approval of vendor invoices for maintenance services.
Item 7.2: Approval of transportation route changes for elementary zones.
Item 7.3: Approval of new faculty employment contracts for the 2023-2024 academic year.

Motion to approve the consent agenda was made by Trustee L. Garcia and seconded by Trustee K. O’Neill.
The roll call vote on the consent agenda was as follows:
Trustee M. Thompson: Aye
Trustee L. Garcia: Aye
Trustee K. O’Neill: Aye
Trustee D. Chen: Aye
Trustee A. Johnson: Aye

The consent agenda was approved.

PUBLIC COMMENT PERIOD (GENERAL)

The public comment period was opened at 6:15 P.M.
Three members of the public signed the registration log.
Speaker 1: Resident of Northwood, Parcel ID 45-001. Comment regarding noise levels on North Street.
Speaker 2: Parent of student, District ID 998-22. Comment regarding bus pickup times.
Speaker 3: Resident of Northwood, Parcel ID 12-005. Comment regarding library hours.

The public comment period closed at 6:30 P.M.

NEW BUSINESS: CONTRACT RATIFICATION

Item 8.1: Discussion and Vote on Teacher Union Contract Ratification.

Superintendent R. Wilson took the podium to present the district position. The Superintendent outlined the budgetary constraints and the necessity of maintaining competitive compensation to retain qualified faculty. The Superintendent stated that the proposed agreement includes a salary increase of 4.5% over the current scale.

Union Representative S. Martinez took the podium to present the union position. The Representative outlined the demands regarding working conditions, class size caps, and compensation. The Representative stated that the proposed agreement secures a term of 3 Years.

Legal Counsel J. Miller provided a legal review of the contract language. The counsel confirmed that the contract language complies with state education code provisions regarding collective bargaining. The counsel noted the effective date is set for 2024-07-01.

Public Comment on Item 8.1 was opened.
The public comment period was scheduled for 45 minutes.
The clock was started at 6:35 P.M.

Public Comment Log Summary:
1.  J. Doe, 123 Main St. Comment: Support for the increase.
2.  M. Roe, 456 Oak Ave. Comment: Concerns regarding property tax implications.
3.  P. Smith, 789 Elm Rd. Comment: Support for the term length.
4.  T. White, 321 Pine Ln. Comment: Concerns regarding non-teacher staff compensation.
5.  K. Black, 654 Cedar Blvd. Comment: Support for the agreement.
6.  R. Green, 987 Birch Way. Comment: Inquiry about professional development funds.
7.  S. Brown, 147 Walnut St. Comment: Support for the ratification.
8.  D. Grey, 258 Spruce Dr. Comment: Concerns regarding retirement contributions.
9.  L. Blue, 369 Ash Ct. Comment: Support for the salary increase.
10. C. Red, 741 Maple Ave. Comment: General support for the district.

The public comment period concluded at 7:20 P.M. Total duration recorded as 45 minutes.

Motion to Ratify Contract:
Trustee K. O’Neill made a motion to ratify the Teacher Union Contract Agreement between Northwood Independent School District and the Northwood Education Association.
Trustee D. Chen seconded the motion.

Discussion ensued regarding the specific terms of compensation and the duration of the agreement. Superintendent R. Wilson addressed questions regarding the 4.5% salary increase. Union Representative S. Martinez addressed questions regarding the 3 Years contract term.

Vote on Motion to Ratify:
The President called for a roll call vote.

Trustee M. Thompson: Aye
Trustee L. Garcia: Aye
Trustee K. O’Neill: Aye
Trustee D. Chen: Aye
Trustee A. Johnson: Nay

The vote result was 4-1.
Motion to Ratify passed.

The Board confirmed that the Contract Agreement shall be signed by Superintendent R. Wilson and Union Representative S. Martinez. The Board confirmed the effective date of the contract is 2024-07-01. The Board confirmed the contract term is 3 Years.

PUBLIC COMMENT PERIOD (SPECIAL)

A special public comment period regarding Item 8.1 was opened at 7:25 P.M.
The Board President announced that this period is dedicated solely to the contract ratification.
The duration was set for 45 minutes.
Speakers registered at the desk were provided with a timer.

Speaker 1: A. Citizen. Comment regarding specific clause 4.2.
Speaker 2: B. Parent. Comment regarding clause 5.1.
Speaker 3: C. Staff. Comment regarding clause 3.0.
Speaker 4: D. Resident. Comment regarding the 4.5% figure.
Speaker 5: E. Union Member. Comment regarding the 3 Years term.
Speaker 6: F. Teacher. Comment regarding the 2024-07-01 date.
Speaker 7: G. Parent. Comment regarding the 4-1 vote.
Speaker 8: H. Citizen. Comment regarding the signatories R. Wilson and S. Martinez.
Speaker 9: I. Teacher. Comment regarding the ratification process.
Speaker 10: J. Resident. Comment regarding the budget.

The public comment period closed at 8:10 P.M.

ADJOURNMENT

Motion to adjourn the meeting was made by Trustee L. Garcia and seconded by Trustee K. O’Neill.
The roll call vote on the adjournment was as follows:
Trustee M. Thompson: Aye
Trustee L. Garcia: Aye
Trustee K. O’Neill: Aye
Trustee D. Chen: Aye
Trustee A. Johnson: Aye

The meeting was adjourned at 8:15 P.M.

RESPECTFULLY SUBMITTED,

J. HARRIS
SECRETARY OF THE BOARD

NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT
CONTRACT AGREEMENT
AGREEMENT NUMBER: NISD-2024-001
DATE OF EXECUTION: 2023-10-12

THIS AGREEMENT is made and entered into this 12th day of October, 2023, by and between NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT, a political subdivision of the State of Texas (hereinafter referred to as the "District"), and the NORTHWOOD EDUCATION ASSOCIATION, a labor organization (hereinafter referred to as the "Association").

WHEREAS, the District desires to employ certified personnel to provide educational services to the students of the District; and

WHEREAS, the Association represents the certified employees of the District; and

WHEREAS, the parties desire to establish the terms and conditions of employment for the certified employees;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

ARTICLE I: DEFINITIONS

1.1 "Certified Employee" shall mean any employee of the District holding a valid teaching certificate issued by the Texas Education Agency.
1.2 "Fiscal Year" shall mean the period from September 1 of any year to August 31 of the following year.
1.3 "Academic Year" shall mean the period from the first day of instruction in September to the last day of instruction in June.
1.4 "Salary Schedule" shall mean the compensation scale attached hereto as Exhibit A.
1.5 "Benefits" shall mean the health, dental, and retirement benefits attached hereto as Exhibit B.

ARTICLE II: TERM

2.1 This Agreement shall become effective on July 1, 2024 (hereinafter referred to as the "Effective Date").
2.2 The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of 3 Years (hereinafter referred to as the "Contract Term").
2.3 The Contract Term shall expire on June 30, 2027.
2.4 Either party may provide written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the Contract Term.
2.5 If no notice of non-renewal is provided, this Agreement shall automatically renew for an additional 1 Year period.

ARTICLE III: COMPENSATION

3.1 Base Salary.
3.1.1 The District shall pay the base salary of each Certified Employee according to the Salary Schedule set forth in Exhibit A.
3.1.2 The Salary Schedule shall reflect a salary increase of 4.5% for all steps on the schedule effective on the Effective Date.
3.1.3 The salary increase shall be prorated for any employee commencing employment after the Effective Date.
3.2 Longevity Pay.
3.2.1 Employees with five (5) or more years of service in the District shall receive a longevity pay increment of $500 per year.
3.2.2 Employees with ten (10) or more years of service in the District shall receive a longevity pay increment of $1,000 per year.
3.3 Stipends.
3.3.1 Stipends for coaching, advising, and department chairperson duties shall be paid in accordance with District Policy 3.10.
3.3.2 Stipends shall be paid on a monthly basis throughout the school year.

ARTICLE IV: WORKING CONDITIONS

4.1 Work Day.
4.1.1 The standard work day for Certified Employees shall be eight (8) hours.
4.1.2 The standard work week shall be five (5) days.
4.2 Class Size.
4.2.1 Class sizes shall not exceed thirty (30) students per classroom for elementary grades.
4.2.2 Class sizes shall not exceed thirty-five (35) students per classroom for secondary grades.
4.3 Planning Time.
4.3.1 Each Certified Employee shall receive at least one (1) planning period per day.
4.3.2 Planning periods shall not be assigned to non-instructional duties.

ARTICLE V: PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT

5.1 District Support.
5.1.1 The District shall allocate funds for professional development activities.
5.1.2 Funds shall be allocated based on the number of Certified Employees employed.
5.2 Leave for Professional Development.
5.2.1 Certified Employees may take up to five (5) days of professional development leave per year.
5.2.2 Professional development leave shall be with full compensation.
5.2.3 Professional development leave must be approved by the Superintendent.

ARTICLE VI: DISPUTE RESOLUTION

6.1 Grievance Procedure.
6.1.1 Grievances shall be processed in accordance with the District Grievance Procedure.
6.1.2 All grievances must be submitted in writing within ten (10) days of the incident.
6.2 Arbitration.
6.2.1 Disputes regarding the interpretation of this Agreement shall be submitted to arbitration.
6.2.2 The arbitrator shall be selected by mutual agreement of the parties.
6.2.3 The decision of the arbitrator shall be final and binding.

ARTICLE VII: TERMINATION

7.1 Termination for Cause.
7.1.1 The District may terminate employment for cause in accordance with state law.
7.1.2 Cause includes insubordination, negligence, or violation of District Policy.
7.2 Termination without Cause.
7.2.1 The District may terminate employment without cause upon payment of severance pay.
7.2.2 Severance pay shall be equal to one (1) month of salary for each year of service.
7.3 Resignation.
7.3.1 Certified Employees shall provide thirty (30) days written notice of resignation.
7.3.2 Failure to provide notice may result in forfeiture of unused sick leave.

ARTICLE VIII: GENERAL PROVISIONS

8.1 Governing Law.
8.1.1 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Texas.
8.2 Severability.
8.2.1 If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid, the remainder shall continue in full force.
8.3 Entire Agreement.
8.3.1 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties.
8.4 Amendments.
8.4.1 Amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties.
8.5 Notices.
8.5.1 All notices shall be sent via certified mail to the addresses listed in this Agreement.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement on the date first written above.

LEGAL OPINION MEMORANDUM
TO: BOARD OF TRUSTEES, NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT
FROM: J. MILLER, LEGAL COUNSEL
DATE: 2023-10-10
SUBJECT: LEGAL REVIEW OF CONTRACT RATIFICATION

This memorandum serves as the legal opinion regarding the proposed Contract Agreement between the District and the Northwood Education Association.

1.0 AUTHORITY TO NEGOTIATE
The District has the authority to negotiate and enter into collective bargaining agreements with its employees pursuant to Texas Education Code Section 21.001 et seq.

2.0 COMPLIANCE WITH STATUTE
The proposed agreement complies with all applicable state statutes. The salary increase of 4.5% is within the budgetary limits approved by the Board. The contract term of 3 Years is consistent with standard practice in the region. The effective date of 2024-07-01 aligns with the fiscal year start.

3.0 FUNDING
The necessary funds for the salary increase and benefits are available in the District budget. The financial impact statement attached hereto confirms the availability of funds.

4.0 PROCEDURAL COMPLIANCE
The negotiation process followed all required public notice procedures. The Board meeting on 2023-10-12 provided the required opportunity for public comment. The vote result of 4-1 meets the threshold for adoption.

5.0 RECOMMENDATION
Based on the review, it is the opinion of Legal Counsel that the Contract Agreement is valid and enforceable. The Board is advised to proceed with ratification.

RESPECTFULLY SUBMITTED,
J. MILLER
LEGAL COUNSEL

FINANCIAL IMPACT STATEMENT
NORTHWOOD INDEPENDENT SCHOOL DISTRICT
DATE: 2023-10-10
SUBJECT: BUDGETARY IMPACT OF CONTRACT RATIFICATION

1.0 SUMMARY
This statement outlines the financial impact of the proposed Contract Agreement on the District budget for the fiscal year 2024-2025.

2.0 CURRENT SALARY EXPENSE
Current total salary expense for certified employees is $15,000,000.

3.0 PROPOSED SALARY EXPENSE
Proposed total salary expense for certified employees is $15,675,000.

4.0 INCREASE
The proposed increase is $675,000. This represents a 4.5% increase over the current baseline.

5.0 BENEFITS EXPENSE
Current total benefits expense is $3,000,000.
Proposed total benefits expense is $3,135,000.
The proposed increase is $135,000.

6.0 TOTAL IMPACT
The total financial impact is $810,000.
This amount is to be funded through the general fund surplus.

7.0 LONG TERM IMPACT
The 3 Years contract term results in a total commitment of $2,430,000 over the life of the agreement.

SUBJECT LINE: FOIA Request - Campaign Finance and Property Records

REQUEST DETAILS
The undersigned requester, acting in the capacity of a journalist for the Millbrook Herald, submits this request pursuant to the State Freedom of Information Act and the Municipal Transparency Ordinance of 2018. The request seeks access to all public records, documents, and data files held by the Municipality of Millbrook, including those held by the Millbrook Ethics Commission and the County Recorder's Office, specifically regarding the following subjects and time periods.

1. CAMPAIGN FINANCE RECORDS
The requester seeks all campaign finance filings, contribution reports, expenditure logs, and donor disclosure forms submitted to the Millbrook Ethics Commission.
Target Entities: All members of the Millbrook Zoning Board of Appeals (ZBA) serving during the period of January 1, 2024, through July 28, 2024.
Specific Focus: All contributions received by ZBA members from entities or individuals with a direct or indirect financial interest in real estate development within the City of Millbrook.
Time Period: January 1, 2024, to July 28, 2024.
Format: Digital copies in searchable PDF format and CSV export of raw data where available.

2. PROPERTY TRANSFER RECORDS
The requester seeks all recorded property transfer deeds, affidavits of ownership, and title transfer documents filed with the County Recorder's Office.
Target Location: All parcels of real property located within a 500-foot radius of the target parcel identified as Parcel ID: 44-2024-MLK-99 (Target Parcel).
Time Period: June 1, 2024, to July 28, 2024.
Format: Certified copies of recorded deeds and digital metadata logs.

3. BUSINESS LICENSING AND ENTITY RECORDS
The requester seeks all business license applications, LLC formation documents, and registered agent filings associated with any entity that has made a contribution to a ZBA member or has conducted a property transfer within the specified 500-foot radius during the requested time period.
Cross Reference: Business Licenses, LLC Ownership, Property Deeds.

REQUESTED OUTPUT FORMAT
The requester prefers electronic delivery via email to the address listed above. A physical copy is to be filed in the public records archive at the FOIA Office if digital transmission fails or if requested by the department.

FEE ASSESSMENT
The requester has indicated a willingness to pay any applicable fees for duplication and processing.
Fee Assessment: $0.00
Reason: The request is being processed under the "High Public Interest" clause of the Municipal Transparency Ordinance, which waives standard duplication fees for media requests involving public safety and ethical compliance.

SECTION II: LEGAL DEPARTMENT MEMORANDUM
